title: Observer can rewrite driveFolderId/pdfDriveFileId on a Draft — including pointing draft-delete cleanup at the district-wide parent folder
currentState: The Draft-update rule freezes status, participant emails, timestamps, and scheduling linkage but NOT driveFolderId, pdfDriveFileId, audioDriveFileIds, transcripts, or evidenceLinks (firestore.rules:264-279). All of those are written exclusively server-side in practice, so client write access is pure attack surface. Worst case: an observer sets driveFolderId to the parent folder ID (committed in the repo at apps/functions/.env.peer-evaluator-rubric:1) and then deletes their Draft — onObservationWritten unconditionally calls deleteDriveFolder(before.driveFolderId) (apps/functions/src/observations/onObservationWritten.ts:285-295), which permanently deletes every child file/folder and then the folder itself (apps/functions/src/lib/drive.ts:151-183, hard delete, no trash). That destroys every observation PDF, audio recording, and evidence file in the district. Lesser variants: pointing driveFolderId at another observation's folder (finalize then shares that folder with the wrong staff member), or spoofing pdfDriveFileId to change where 'View PDF' buttons send the observed staff member.
expectedBehavior: Server-managed Drive linkage fields are immutable from the client (like windowId/slotId already are), and the server-side folder deletion verifies the folder actually belongs to this observation tree before permanently deleting it.
evidence: firestore.rules:264-279 (unchanged() list omits driveFolderId/pdfDriveFileId/audioDriveFileIds/transcripts/evidenceLinks); apps/functions/src/observations/onObservationWritten.ts:285-295 (deletes before.driveFolderId unconditionally); apps/functions/src/lib/drive.ts:151-183 (permanent recursive delete); apps/functions/.env.peer-evaluator-rubric:1 (parent folder ID is public in-repo); evidence/audio/transcripts written only server-side (uploadEvidenceFile.ts:151-155, uploadAudio.ts:130-134, onTranscriptionJobCreated.ts:115-118)
implementationPlan: firestore.rules: extend the observer Draft-update branch with unchanged('driveFolderId') && unchanged('pdfDriveFileId') && unchanged('audioDriveFileIds') && unchanged('transcripts') && unchanged('evidenceLinks') (evidence/audio go through callables, so the web autosave at ObservationEditorPage.tsx flush() is unaffected — it only writes observationData/componentNotes/scriptDoc/meeting fields/name/date). Defense in depth in deleteDriveFolder: before deleting, fetch the folder's parents and require parents includes DRIVE_PARENT_FOLDER_ID (pass it in from onObservationWritten); refuse and log otherwise. Add rules tests in tests/rules/observations.test.ts asserting an observer's Draft update changing driveFolderId/pdfDriveFileId is denied, and a unit test for the parent check.

title: Archiving/deactivating a staff member does not revoke their access — isActive is ignored in claim computation
currentState: The admin Staff page 'Archive' action sets only { isActive: false } on the staff doc (apps/web/src/admin/staff/StaffPage.tsx:343), leaving role/hasAdminAccess intact. Both claim-sync paths compute claims purely from role + hasAdminAccess and never read isActive: syncMyClaims.ts:39-42 and onStaffWritten.ts:41-44. The onStaffWritten docstring (lines 16-17) claims 'admin deactivates a staff member — role goes to null, hasSpecialAccess to false' but the code does not do that. firestore.rules contains no isActive check anywhere (grep confirms zero matches). Result: an archived Peer Evaluator/Administrator keeps hasSpecialAccess/isAdmin claims, can still sign in, list all staff, read every observation, and (if admin) write every admin collection.
expectedBehavior: Archiving a staff member is an access-revocation event: claims should collapse to { role: null, hasSpecialAccess: false, isAdmin: false } when isActive === false, on both the trigger path and the callable path, and the stale docstring should be corrected. Optionally Firebase Auth user disabled on archive.
evidence: apps/functions/src/auth/syncMyClaims.ts:37-44 (no isActive read); apps/functions/src/auth/onStaffWritten.ts:41-44 (no isActive read) and 16-17 (docstring contradicts code); apps/web/src/admin/staff/StaffPage.tsx:343 (archive = isActive:false only); firestore.rules (no isActive reference anywhere)
implementationPlan: 1) Extract a shared computeClaims(staffData) helper in apps/functions/src/auth/computeClaims.ts: if (!staffData || staffData.isActive === false) return { role: null, hasSpecialAccess: false, isAdmin: false }; else current logic (treat missing isActive as active for legacy docs). 2) Use it in both syncMyClaims.ts and onStaffWritten.ts; fix the onStaffWritten docstring. 3) Unit-test the helper (archived PE, archived admin, hasAdminAccess+archived, missing isActive). 4) Optional hardening: in onStaffWritten, when access was revoked (special/admin -> none), call getAuth().revokeRefreshTokens(user.uid) so the old token dies within the hour instead of lingering. 5) Verify no fixture/staff doc lacks isActive in a way that would lock out active users (default missing -> active).

title: Staff-facing module page item lists are denied by Firestore rules (resources/materials always render empty for staff)
currentState: ModulePage subscribes to /modules/{id}/items with NO query constraints (apps/web/src/modules/ModulePage.tsx:36-38). The collection-group rule (firestore.rules:137-143) only allows non-admin reads when `resource.data.moduleId in staffDocData().modules` (or auto-enable matches). Firestore evaluates list queries against the query, not the documents — an unfiltered list cannot be proven to satisfy a resource.data condition, so the query is rejected for every non-admin user. The error is also silently swallowed (ModulePage destructures only `data`, not `error`), so assigned staff see 'No resources yet' / 'No materials yet' in every resources/materials section. Admins pass `isAdmin()` so the bug is invisible during admin testing. The rules tests (tests/rules/modules.test.ts:231-292) only cover single gets and the dashboard's filtered collectionGroup query — there is no test for the module page's direct unfiltered subcollection list.
expectedBehavior: An assigned staff member opening /m/{moduleId} sees every resource and material the admin published. The items query must be provably authorized: include where('moduleId','==',moduleId) so the rule's `resource.data.moduleId in staffDocData().modules || matchesModuleAutoEnable(...)` condition is evaluable. Query errors should surface in the UI instead of masquerading as empty sections.
evidence: apps/web/src/modules/ModulePage.tsx:36-38 (unconstrained list, error not destructured); firestore.rules:137-143 (read gated on resource.data.moduleId); tests/rules/modules.test.ts:250-263 (only the filtered collectionGroup path is tested)
implementationPlan: 1) In ModulePage.tsx pass constraints [where('moduleId','==',moduleId)] (plus keyParts [moduleId]) to useFirestoreCollection for the items subscription. 2) Destructure and render the items `error` (reuse the error banner pattern from ModulesPage). 3) Add rules tests: (a) assigned staff CAN list /modules/{id}/items WITH where('moduleId','==',id); (b) assigned staff CANNOT list it unfiltered; (c) auto-enable-matched staff can list with the filter. 4) Run pnpm test:rules (TEMP/TMP=C:/Temp + Java 21).

title: Booking page shows ALL active signup fields instead of the window's selected fields — hard-fails day-preference submissions
currentState: PEs pick which signup fields apply per window (CreateObservationWindowDialog → window.signupFieldIds, persisted by createObservationWindow). BookingPage ignores this: it loads the whole /signupFields collection and renders every active field matching the mode. In day-preference mode, submitDayPreference rejects any answer whose fieldId is not in window.signupFieldIds, so an active+required field that the PE did NOT select makes the page demand an answer and then the server throws 'Unknown signup field' — staff cannot submit at all. In direct mode, unselected fields are silently collected and stored (bookObservationSlot performs no membership check).
expectedBehavior: BookingPage filters fields to windowDoc.signupFieldIds (intersected with isActive/appliesTo), required-gating only considers those fields, and both callables validate answers against the window's field list consistently.
evidence: apps/web/src/scheduling/BookingPage.tsx:86 (loads full collection) and :106 (uses it unfiltered; never references windowDoc.signupFieldIds); apps/web/src/observations/CreateObservationWindowDialog.tsx:110,240-247,314 (per-window selection); apps/functions/src/scheduling/createObservationWindow.ts:143 (signupFieldIds stored); apps/functions/src/scheduling/submitDayPreference.ts:91-96 (rejects unknown fieldIds); apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:192-196,299-307 (no membership validation, stores input.detailAnswers verbatim)
implementationPlan: Web: in BookingPage compute fields = (signupFields ?? []).filter(f => windowDoc.signupFieldIds.includes(f.fieldId)) before passing to SignupDetailFields/signupFieldsComplete/buildDetailAnswers. Functions: add the same membership check in bookObservationSlot that submitDayPreference already has. Tests: unit test for the filtering helper; functions test for unknown-field rejection on bookObservationSlot.

title: Manually created observations have no observationId field, breaking dashboard CTAs and Acknowledge
currentState: CreateObservationDialog.create() addDoc()s the observation without an `observationId` field (apps/web/src/observations/CreateObservationDialog.tsx:67-90), while the booking path sets it (apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:93). The staff dashboard's step interpreter builds CTA links and the acknowledge action from `obs.observationId` (apps/web/src/dashboard/deriveCheckpoints.ts:84 and :92). For manually created observations that field is undefined, so 'observation'-target steps link to "/observations/undefined" and the Acknowledge button never renders (DashboardView.tsx:565 requires task.ackObservationId), making sign-off unreachable for every manually created observation.
expectedBehavior: Every observation doc carries its own id (schema requires observationId: z.string().min(1)); dashboard CTAs resolve to a valid /observations/{id} URL and the Acknowledge button works regardless of how the observation was created.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/CreateObservationDialog.tsx:67-90 (no observationId in payload); apps/web/src/dashboard/deriveCheckpoints.ts:84,92 (uses obs.observationId); apps/web/src/dashboard/DashboardView.tsx:565 (ack button gated on task.ackObservationId); packages/shared/src/schema/observation.ts:66 (field required by schema)
implementationPlan: 1) In CreateObservationDialog, switch addDoc to doc(collection(db, COLLECTIONS.observations)) + setDoc and include observationId: ref.id. 2) Harden deriveCheckpoints/resolveButton to fall back to the snapshot doc id (the hooks already attach `id`): use obs.observationId || obs.id. 3) Add a one-off backfill (admin script function like migrateRolesToSlugs) that stamps observationId = doc.id on existing docs. 4) Extend deriveCheckpoints.test.ts with an observation lacking observationId.

title: roleYearMapping.updated emails can never reach staff (slug-vs-displayName query + static mail doc id)
currentState: onRoleYearMappingWritten queries staff with where('role','==', roleDisplayName) (apps/functions/src/settings/onRoleYearMappingWritten.ts:36-41), but staff.role stores the role SLUG, not the displayName — packages/shared/src/roles.ts:8-9 documents this and apps/functions/src/scripts/migrateRolesToSlugs.ts converts staff.role to slugs. The query therefore matches zero staff and the 'Assigned Subdomains Notification' template (seeded active, scripts/import/seed.ts:131-147) never sends. Additionally the mail doc id is static per mapping+staff ('subdomains-${mappingId}-${staffEmail}', onRoleYearMappingWritten.ts:67), and the Trigger Email extension only sends on /mail doc CREATION (acknowledged in apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:27-35), so even after fixing the query, any second update to the same role/year mapping would silently no-op.
expectedBehavior: When an admin updates subdomain assignments for a role/year, every active staff member in that role+year receives the notification email — including on subsequent re-assignments in later cycles.
evidence: apps/functions/src/settings/onRoleYearMappingWritten.ts:36-41 (where('role','==', roleDisplayName)); onRoleYearMappingWritten.ts:67 (static mailDocId); packages/shared/src/roles.ts:8-9 (staff.role stores the slug); apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:27-35 (extension sends on doc creation only)
implementationPlan: 1) Change the staff query to where('role','==', roleId) (the slug from the mapping doc), keeping a displayName fallback for un-migrated docs if desired. 2) Make the mail doc id unique per send, e.g. `subdomains-${mappingId}-${staffEmail.split('@')[0]}-${Date.now()}` (mirrors staffInviteMailDocId rationale). 3) Add a unit test with a stubbed Firestore asserting the query field and a changing doc id across two invocations. 4) Verify with emulator fixtures that an update to roleYearMappings/{id} produces /mail docs.

title: Profile 'My Administrators' card can never show administrators (rules deny + missing index)
currentState: ProfilePage queries the staff collection with [where('role','==','administrator'), where('isActive','==',true), orderBy('name')] for every signed-in user. Firestore rules only allow `list` on /staff for special-access users (firestore.rules:93, intent confirmed by tests/rules/auth.test.ts:65-67 'blocks teachers from listing staff'), so for teachers — the card's primary audience — the listener errors with permission-denied. Even for special-access users the query needs a staff composite index (role+isActive+name) that does not exist in firestore.indexes.json. The error is never surfaced (ProfilePage does not destructure `error`), so everyone sees the misleading 'No administrator on file for your building'.
expectedBehavior: Every staff member sees the administrator(s) assigned to their building(s) on their profile, with a visible error state if the lookup fails.
evidence: apps/web/src/routes/ProfilePage.tsx:31-35,167-170,185-189,303-306; firestore.rules:91-94; tests/rules/auth.test.ts:65-67; firestore.indexes.json:1-122 (no staff composite index)
implementationPlan: 1) firestore.rules: add a narrowly-scoped list clause on /staff, e.g. `allow list: if hasSpecialAccess() || (isFromOronoDomain() && resource.data.role == 'administrator' && resource.data.isActive == true);` — Firestore proves the query filters guarantee the condition. 2) ProfilePage: drop orderBy('name') from ADMIN_CONSTRAINTS (equality-only avoids a new composite index, matching the codebase convention) and sort client-side; destructure and render the query `error`. 3) tests/rules/auth.test.ts: add cases — teacher CAN list staff filtered to role==administrator && isActive==true; teacher still CANNOT list unfiltered staff.

title: Peer evaluators cannot use the Send Email feature — rules block their template read
currentState: StaffPersonPage (a special-access route, apps/web/src/App.tsx:106) drives its Send Email dropdown from a client query on /emailTemplates (apps/web/src/routes/StaffPersonPage.tsx:41-45, 105-108), but firestore.rules:165-169 restrict /emailTemplates read+write to isAdmin(), and peer-evaluator is special-access but NOT admin (packages/shared/src/roles.ts:60-70). The rules tests even assert 'PE cannot read' (tests/rules/admin-collections.test.ts:115-118). So for the primary persona — a PE — the query is permission-denied, manualTemplates stays null, hasTemplates is false, and the Send Email button is permanently disabled with 'No active manual templates' (StaffPersonPage.tsx:273-274). Meanwhile the sendManualEmail callable explicitly allows PEs (apps/functions/src/email/sendManualEmail.ts:30-33: 'Only PEs and admins can send manual emails').
expectedBehavior: A PE on a staff member's page can pick an active manual template and send it, matching the callable's permission model.
evidence: firestore.rules:165-169 (allow read,write: if isAdmin()); apps/web/src/routes/StaffPersonPage.tsx:41-45 + 273-274 (query + disabled button); apps/functions/src/email/sendManualEmail.ts:30-33 (PEs allowed to send); tests/rules/admin-collections.test.ts:115-118 (PE read asserted to fail)
implementationPlan: 1) firestore.rules: split /emailTemplates to `allow read: if hasSpecialAccess(); allow write: if isAdmin();` (templates contain no sensitive data; read is needed to populate the picker). 2) Update tests/rules/admin-collections.test.ts: PE read should now succeed, PE write still fails; run pnpm test:rules (TEMP=C:/Temp + Java 21). 3) Optionally surface the collection error state in StaffPersonPage instead of silently disabling the button.

title: Cancelling a window strands booked slots/invitees and sends no cancellation emails despite UI claiming it does
currentState: cancelObservationWindow blocks only NON-booked slots, deletes the Draft observations behind booked slots (which removes their Google Calendar events), but never frees the booked slots, never clears invitees[].bookedSlotId or peBusyIntervals, and sends zero emails — while the MyObservationWindowsPage confirm prompt explicitly says 'This notifies invitees.' Staff with bookings have their calendar events silently deleted with no notification, and the window doc is left claiming they are still booked.
expectedBehavior: Window cancellation tears down every booking consistently (slot → blocked/window-cancelled, invitee bookedSlotId cleared, ledger cleared), and sends scheduling.bookingCancelled emails to every invitee with a booking (plus optionally a window-cancelled notice to non-booked invitees), honoring cancellationEmailEnabled.
evidence: apps/functions/src/scheduling/cancelObservationWindow.ts:68-101 (booked slots skipped at line 74, no invitee/ledger reset, no sendTemplatedEmail anywhere); apps/web/src/observations/MyObservationWindowsPage.tsx:100-103 (prompt text 'This notifies invitees')
implementationPlan: In cancelObservationWindow: capture booked slots before blocking, also flip booked slots to blocked/window-cancelled, update the window doc to clear invitees[].bookedSlotId and peBusyIntervals, and after draft cleanup loop send scheduling.bookingCancelled (vars from each booked slot) to bookedBy + observer gated on cancellationEmailEnabled; optionally add a 'scheduling.windowCancelled' trigger for non-booked invitees (add to EMAIL_TRIGGER_TYPES + seed template + admin TRIGGER_LABELS). Add tests for the teardown path.

title: Deleting a booked Draft observation strands the slot, ledger, invitee state, and Google Calendar events
currentState: firestore.rules lets the observer delete a Draft observation client-side, and StaffPersonPage does exactly that with deleteDoc. The onObservationWritten deletion handler cleans up only the Drive folder and the Sheets row — it does not free the booked slot, remove the peBusyIntervals entry, clear the invitee's bookedSlotId, or delete the gcalEventIds events. Result: the slot stays 'booked' pointing at a deleted observation, the invitee can never rebook in that window, and both parties keep ghost calendar events forever.
expectedBehavior: Deleting a scheduling-linked Draft tears the booking down exactly like cancelBooking does: slot freed, ledger and invitee reset, blocked slots recomputed, calendar events deleted, parties notified — or client-side deletion of scheduling-linked observations is blocked and routed through cancelBooking.
evidence: firestore.rules:296-300 (observer may delete a Draft); apps/web/src/routes/StaffPersonPage.tsx:146-154 (raw deleteDoc on observations); apps/functions/src/observations/onObservationWritten.ts:277-296 (handleDeletion: Drive + Sheet only — no slot/ledger/invitee/gcal cleanup, unlike scheduling/draftCleanup.ts which handles gcal but is only called from cancel paths)
implementationPlan: Extend onObservationWritten's handleDeletion: when beforeData has windowId/slotId, run the cancelBooking reversal logic (factor the transaction body out of cancelBooking into a shared helper in scheduling/, then call it + deleteObservationEvent for both gcalEventIds entries). Alternatively (preferred, simpler invariants) tighten firestore.rules to deny observer deletes when resource.data.windowId != null and change StaffPersonPage to call cancelBooking for scheduling-linked drafts. Add a rules test for the new restriction.

title: Evaluator can be double-booked across overlapping windows (busy ledger is per-window only)
currentState: The PE-busy ledger (peBusyIntervals) lives on each observationWindow doc, and bookObservationSlot/assignObservationFromPreference only check conflicts against the CURRENT window's ledger (bookObservationSlot.ts:259, assignObservationFromPreference.ts:117). If a PE opens two windows with overlapping date ranges (e.g. one per building, or a direct window plus a day-preference window), bookings in one window never block conflicting slots in the other — the evaluator's wall-clock time can be booked twice. createObservationWindow also never warns about an overlapping existing window.
expectedBehavior: The booking/assignment transaction checks the evaluator's busy intervals across ALL of their open/partially-booked windows (± travel buffer), recomputeBlockedSlots blocks slots that conflict with sibling-window bookings, and window creation warns the PE when the new date range overlaps an existing active window of theirs.
evidence: packages/shared/src/schema/observationWindow.ts:14-18 (ledger documented as per-window); apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:259; apps/functions/src/scheduling/assignObservationFromPreference.ts:117; apps/functions/src/scheduling/engine/blocking.ts:36-44 (only reads the one window's ledger); no collectionGroup/observerEmail cross-query anywhere in apps/functions/src/scheduling/
implementationPlan: In bookObservationSlot and assignObservationFromPreference transactions, add a query for sibling windows (where observerEmail == window.observerEmail, status in [open, partially-booked]) and run peConflicts against the union of their peBusyIntervals (Admin SDK transactions support queries). Extend recomputeBlockedSlots to accept the merged ledger and call it for every affected sibling window after a booking/cancel. Add a composite index (observerEmail, status) to firestore.indexes.json if not present. In createObservationWindow, detect overlapping active windows for the same observer and include a warning in the response; surface it in CreateObservationWindowDialog. Unit tests for merged-ledger conflict logic in engine tests.

title: Observation date and finalized date render as "—" in every generated PDF
currentState: finalizeObservation POSTs the raw Firestore snapshot to the Cloud Run renderer via gaxios JSON serialization (apps/functions/src/lib/pdfRenderer.ts:36-43). Admin SDK Timestamps serialize to {"_seconds":...,"_nanoseconds":...} (verified empirically against the installed @google-cloud/firestore@7.11.6). The renderer's formatDate (apps/pdf-renderer/src/template.ts:209-222) only handles Date | string | {toDate()}, so the deserialized plain object falls through to '—'. The cover's "Observation date" row therefore always prints "—". Additionally, the payload's observation is the PRE-claim data returned by claimObservationForFinalize (apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeClaim.ts:36-52), where finalizedAt is still null, so the "Finalized" row (template.ts:42, 167) never renders at all.
expectedBehavior: The permanent PDF record shows the actual observation date and the finalization date — these are the two most important metadata fields on an official peer-observation artifact.
evidence: apps/pdf-renderer/src/template.ts:209-222 (formatDate misses the {_seconds} shape); apps/functions/src/lib/pdfRenderer.ts:36-43 (JSON POST of raw Timestamps); apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeObservation.ts:118-124 (payload built from pre-claim obs); apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeClaim.ts:47-52 (returns pre-update data, finalizedAt null)
implementationPlan: In finalizeObservation, normalize the payload at the boundary: convert observationDate, finalizedAt, preObsDate, postObsDate (and any driveFileRef.uploadedAt) to ISO strings before POSTing (a small toIso(value: Timestamp|Date|null) helper), and pass finalizedAt explicitly as new Date().toISOString() since the claim already stamped it server-side. Defensively, also teach template.ts formatDate to handle the {_seconds,_nanoseconds} and {seconds,nanoseconds} shapes. Add a template unit test asserting a Timestamp-shaped and ISO-string date both render as a real date (requires the pdf-renderer test harness from the no-tests gap, or a minimal vitest setup added here).

title: Module edit dialog is dead code — name, description, color, active flag, and auto-enable rule cannot be changed after creation
currentState: ModulesPage declares `const [editing, setEditing] = useState<ModuleRow | null>(null)` (apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModulesPage.tsx:62) and renders an edit-mode ModuleDialog gated on `editing !== null` (lines 181-188), but `setEditing` is never called anywhere — the row 'Edit' action (lines 169-171) and row click (line 152) both navigate to the builder instead. The builder (ModuleBuilderPage.tsx:104-130) only exposes hasPage, icon, and sections. Result: displayName, description, color, isActive, and autoEnable are write-once at creation; the dialog's in-dialog delete confirmation flow (ModulesPage.tsx:495-533) is equally unreachable.
expectedBehavior: Admins can edit every module property after creation: rename, re-describe, recolor, toggle Active, and change/remove the auto-enable rule — either via an 'Edit details' row action opening the existing dialog, or via a details panel on the builder page.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModulesPage.tsx:62 (setEditing declared), 169-171 (Edit navigates instead of setEditing), 181-188 (dialog gated on never-set state); apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleBuilderPage.tsx:104-130 (builder edits only hasPage/icon/sections)
implementationPlan: 1) In ModulesPage rowActions add 'Edit details' → setEditing(r) (keep 'Open builder' as the row click). 2) Also reset the dialog form state on `existing` change — the current `if (open && form.moduleId !== ...)` render-time sync (lines 257-268) only fires when moduleId differs, so stale field values persist if the same row is reopened after a failed save; move to a useEffect keyed on existing/open. 3) Alternatively (or additionally) surface displayName/description/color/isActive/autoEnable editing at the top of ModuleBuilderPage so everything is in one place. 4) Component test: open edit dialog, change color/autoEnable, assert setDoc payload.

title: Transcription job status/failures never reach the UI — failed jobs spin forever
currentState: The schema documents that the client should onSnapshot the job doc (packages/shared/src/schema/transcriptionJob.ts:12) and firestore.rules:321-322 already grants the requester `get`, but the web app never reads /transcriptionJobs at all (zero references in apps/web/src). AudioRecorder discards the returned jobId (AudioRecorder.tsx:75), tracks progress with a local optimistic Set, and only clears the 'Transcribing…' spinner when observation.transcripts[fileId] appears (AudioRecorder.tsx:88-99). If the job fails (Gemini error, bad API key, quota), transcripts never updates, so the spinner shows forever with the disabled button and the job's `error` field is never shown. The re-transcribe path is also broken: because transcripts[fileId] already exists, the clearing effect fires on the very next render, so the spinner disappears instantly and the user gets no signal when (or whether) the re-transcription completed. The optimistic state is also lost on page refresh — a Running job renders as 'no transcript yet'. transcriptPreview and error fields on the job doc are written by the worker but consumed by nothing.
expectedBehavior: After requesting transcription, the client subscribes to /transcriptionJobs/{jobId} via onSnapshot and renders Pending/Running/Completed/Failed states per recording, including the error message and a Retry button on Failed. On mount it queries for an in-flight job per audio file (or stores the active jobId on the observation) so a refresh doesn't lose the in-progress indicator. Re-transcribe shows live progress driven by the job doc, not by transcript presence.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/AudioRecorder.tsx:75 (jobId discarded), AudioRecorder.tsx:88-99 (clears only when transcripts[id] truthy), AudioRecorder.tsx:384-391 (spinner has no failure state), packages/shared/src/schema/transcriptionJob.ts:12 (documented onSnapshot design, unimplemented), firestore.rules:321-323 (requester get already allowed; list is admin-only so an in-flight query needs a rules tweak or jobId persistence)
implementationPlan: 1) New hook apps/web/src/hooks/useTranscriptionJob.ts: given a jobId, onSnapshot the doc and expose {status, error, transcriptPreview}. 2) AudioRecorder: keep returned jobId in state keyed by audioFileId; render status from the job doc; on status==='Failed' show the error and a Retry button that calls requestTranscription again. 3) Refresh resilience: either widen firestore.rules transcriptionJobs `list` to `isCurrentUserEmail(resource.data.requestedBy)` so the client can query in-flight jobs for the observation on mount, or have requestTranscription stamp activeTranscriptionJobIds on the observation doc. 4) Tests: component test for Failed rendering/retry, rules test for requester list/get.

title: Dashboard is not scoped to the current school year and never resets for a second observation
currentState: The finalized-observations query has no date bound (StaffDashboardPage.tsx:107-118 — all finalized ever, newest first), and resolveObservation prefers a finalized observation over an active draft for 'standard' (dashboardEvents.ts:54-57, codified in dashboardEvents.test.ts:41-49). Consequences: (a) a teacher with a finalized observation from a PREVIOUS year opens this year's dashboard and sees every standard checkpoint already done plus 'Cycle complete — nice work' (DashboardView.tsx:317-318) with zero activity this year; (b) probationary staff who get 2-3 observations per year never see signup/preObs/observation/postObs cards track the second draft — those steps stay pinned to the first finalized observation while only the anyDraft reviewDraft card follows the new one; (c) with two unacknowledged finalized observations only the newest gets an acknowledge card.
expectedBehavior: The hero/eyebrow labels say 'Summative cycle · 2025—2026', so checkpoints should derive from the current school year's observations, and a newly created draft should restart the standard-step sequence for the new cycle.

title: Worker death leaves jobs Pending/Running forever and permanently blocks re-transcription
currentState: onTranscriptionJobCreated sets status='Running' outside the try/catch (onTranscriptionJobCreated.ts:69) and runs with no retry config; if the instance dies (timeout — waitForGeminiFileActive alone can burn 5 of the 9 minutes — OOM, crash before the catch), the job is stranded in Running (or Pending if the trigger errors early) with no terminal transition. requestTranscription's idempotency check returns any Pending/Running job regardless of age (requestTranscription.ts:71-85), so that audio file can never be transcribed again — every click returns the dead job. pruneOrphanGeminiFiles only deletes the leaked Gemini file and nulls geminiFileUri (pruneOrphanGeminiFiles.ts:70-73); it never fails the stale job. The duplicate-check is also a non-transactional query+create, so two rapid requests can create duplicate jobs.
expectedBehavior: Jobs always reach a terminal state. A sweep marks Pending/Running jobs older than a threshold (e.g. 1 hour, far past the 9-minute function timeout) as Failed with error='Worker timed out', which unblocks re-requests and lets the UI show failure. The in-flight check ignores jobs older than the threshold. Job creation uses a deterministic ID (observationId_audioFileId_attempt) or a transaction so concurrent requests can't double-create.
evidence: apps/functions/src/transcription/onTranscriptionJobCreated.ts:41-48 (no retry option) and :69 (Running set before try), apps/functions/src/transcription/requestTranscription.ts:71-85 (age-blind in-flight check), apps/functions/src/transcription/pruneOrphanGeminiFiles.ts:70-73 (clears file URI but leaves status Running)
implementationPlan: 1) Extend pruneOrphanGeminiFiles (or new scheduled fn sweepStaleTranscriptionJobs in the same file/folder, exported from index.ts) to query status in ['Pending','Running'] with createdAt < now-1h and update them to Failed with completedAt + error. Run hourly rather than daily so users aren't blocked for a day. 2) In requestTranscription, add a createdAt cutoff to the in-flight query (or check age in code) so stale jobs don't suppress new ones. 3) Wrap the inflight-check + create in a transaction or use deterministic job IDs. 4) Unit tests for the sweep cutoff logic and the in-flight age filter (firestore emulator or extracted pure helpers).

title: Gemini auto-tag writes scriptDoc server-side but the open editor never re-hydrates, then autosave erases the tags
currentState: geminiTagScript updates observation.scriptDoc directly in Firestore (apps/functions/src/observations/geminiTagScript.ts:153-158). The editor hydrates its local draft exactly once per observation id and intentionally ignores later snapshots (apps/web/src/hooks/useHydratedDraft.ts:26-36; ObservationEditorPage.tsx:197-215). So after clicking Auto-tag the user sees the 'Gemini tagged N spans' banner (ScriptEditor.tsx:201-210) but no tags appear in the editor, and the next debounced flush writes the stale local scriptDoc back (ObservationEditorPage.tsx:222-237), permanently deleting the server-applied tags.
expectedBehavior: Auto-tag results appear in the open script editor immediately and survive subsequent autosaves.
evidence: apps/functions/src/observations/geminiTagScript.ts:153-158; apps/web/src/hooks/useHydratedDraft.ts:26-36; apps/web/src/observations/ObservationEditorPage.tsx:197-237; apps/web/src/observations/ScriptEditor.tsx:164-177
implementationPlan: Return the tagged doc from the callable ({ taggedCount, skippedCount, scriptDoc }) and have ScriptEditor.runAutoTag apply it via editor.commands.setContent + onChange(newDoc) so the local draft (and next flush) carries the tags. Alternatively pass the current local scriptDoc up to the callable and tag that. Add a function unit test asserting the response shape and a web test that runAutoTag updates the draft.

title: Work-product/IR question queries need a composite index that is not declared — forms silently show 'No questions configured'
currentState: All four staff/observer question surfaces query workProductQuestions with two equality filters plus orderBy('order') (WorkProductAnswerForm.tsx:20-24, InstructionalRoundAnswerForm.tsx:20-24, WorkProductResponseViewer.tsx:6-10, InstructionalRoundResponseViewer.tsx:6-10). That shape requires a composite index, but firestore.indexes.json declares no workProductQuestions index at all. The emulator doesn't enforce indexes, so dev never sees it; if the prod index was only ever created ad-hoc from a console error link it is config drift that an index deploy can drop. Worse, every consumer destructures only `data` and discards `error`, so an index failure (or any rules/network error) renders as the misleading empty state 'No questions configured yet.'
expectedBehavior: The composite index ships in firestore.indexes.json so fresh environments work, and query errors render as a visible error state instead of an empty question list.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/WorkProductAnswerForm.tsx:20-24,32-35; apps/web/src/observations/InstructionalRoundAnswerForm.tsx:20-24; apps/web/src/observations/WorkProductResponseViewer.tsx:6-10; apps/web/src/observations/InstructionalRoundResponseViewer.tsx:6-10; firestore.indexes.json (no workProductQuestions entry)
implementationPlan: Add to firestore.indexes.json: collectionGroup workProductQuestions, fields [type ASC, isActive ASC, order ASC] (serves both WP and IR variants since both use the same equality fields). In the two forms and two viewers, destructure `error` from useFirestoreCollection and render the standard red error banner when set, distinguishing it from the genuine zero-questions state.

title: Every section edit rewrites the whole sections array; the debounced rich-text flush uses a stale mount-time closure that can silently wipe newer section changes
currentState: All section mutations funnel through setSections → setDoc of the entire array (apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleBuilderPage.tsx:55-65). ModuleSectionEditor debounces body writes 400ms (ModuleSectionEditor.tsx:114-124) and flushes on unmount via an effect with an empty dependency array (lines 101-112, with an eslint-disable) — that cleanup captures the FIRST render's onPatchSection, whose closure holds the mount-time sections array. If an admin types in a rich-text body and navigates away (or any pending 400ms write fires after another section was added/renamed/moved), the write resurrects the stale array, silently deleting newer sections or reverting titles/moves. The same last-write-wins-whole-array design means two admins editing the same module concurrently clobber each other's sections.
expectedBehavior: Section writes never lose concurrent or interleaved edits: patches are computed against the latest persisted state (functional update via a latest-sections ref, a Firestore transaction, or per-section documents), and the unmount flush uses current refs rather than mount-time closures.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleSectionEditor.tsx:101-112 (empty-dep unmount flush over stale closure, eslint-disabled exhaustive-deps), 114-124 (400ms window); apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleBuilderPage.tsx:55-65 (whole-array setDoc per patch)
implementationPlan: 1) In ModuleBuilderPage keep a ref to the latest sections (updated each render) and make patchSection/moveSection/deleteSection operate on ref.current, or wrap section writes in runTransaction reading the module doc. 2) In ModuleSectionEditor, store onPatchSection and section.id in refs the unmount effect reads, removing the eslint-disable. 3) Consider migrating richtext bodies out of the array into per-section docs (aligns with the content-visibility gap) for true conflict isolation. 4) Unit test the flush path (fake timers: type, add section, unmount, assert no section loss).

title: PDF renderer caches a failed/crashed Chromium forever — all finalizations 500 until the container is recycled
currentState: getBrowser() uses `browserPromise ??= puppeteer.launch(...)` (apps/pdf-renderer/src/index.ts:52-69). If the launch promise rejects (transient OOM, cold-start race), the rejected promise stays cached and every subsequent /render-observation request rejects until Cloud Run happens to recycle the instance. Likewise, if Chromium crashes after a successful launch, the cached Browser is disconnected and browser.newPage() throws on every request — there is no `browser.connected` check or relaunch-on-disconnect. The route also has no app.onError, so failures surface as Hono's default opaque 500; the calling function then rolls the observation back to Draft (finalizeObservation.ts:125-128, 218-231), so the user-visible symptom is finalize failing repeatedly with 'PDF rendering failed.'
expectedBehavior: Browser launch failures are not cached: a rejected launch clears the singleton so the next request retries; a disconnected browser triggers relaunch; render errors return structured JSON errors with logging so the function logs identify the cause.
evidence: apps/pdf-renderer/src/index.ts:52-69 (browserPromise ??= with no rejection cleanup, no connected check); apps/pdf-renderer/src/index.ts:29-50 (no onError/structured error handling); apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeObservation.ts:125-128 (caller surfaces a generic 'PDF rendering failed.')
implementationPlan: In getBrowser(): attach `.catch` to clear browserPromise on rejection before rethrowing; after awaiting, if !browser.connected, null the singleton and relaunch once. Add `app.onError` returning JSON {error, message} with console.error so Cloud Run logs carry the stack. Optionally add a per-request render timeout (page.setContent/page.pdf already bounded by Cloud Run's 120s, but an explicit 60s timeout keeps a wedged page from pinning the instance). Unit-test getBrowser's rejection-reset behavior with a mocked puppeteer.launch.

title: Most callables gate 'admin' on the role slug only, ignoring the isAdmin/hasSpecialAccess claims that rules, storage and UI honor
currentState: The privilege model defines admin as `isAdminRole(role) || hasAdminAccess` and mints isAdmin/hasSpecialAccess custom claims (syncMyClaims.ts:41-42). firestore.rules (line 47), storage.rules (lines 11-16, with an explicit comment 'admin = the isAdmin custom claim, not the role slug'), route guards, and the whole web UI use the claims. But these callables re-derive admin from the role slug alone, so a staff member with hasAdminAccess=true and a non-special role (the documented 'dev user' pattern, DevModeContext.tsx:60-63) passes every guard and rules check yet gets permission-denied at the function layer: finalizeObservation.ts:65-66, cancelObservationWindow.ts:52-53, cancelBooking.ts:43-44, assignObservationFromPreference.ts:49-50, geminiTagScript.ts:71-72, backfillScriptTagColors.ts:39-40, sendManualEmail.ts:29-30 (hasSpecialAccess from role only), createObservationWindow.ts:47-48 (same). requestTranscription.ts:64-65 has no admin path at all (observer-only), unlike its sibling geminiTagScript which allows admins. Only uploadEvidenceFile.ts:93 and migrateRolesToSlugs.ts:42 get it right by reading the staff doc.
expectedBehavior: One authorization helper, one definition of admin/special access, used by every callable — a user the rules and UI treat as admin is admin everywhere, and parallel features (transcription vs tagging) have consistent admin override semantics.
evidence: apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeObservation.ts:65-66; apps/functions/src/scheduling/cancelObservationWindow.ts:52-53; apps/functions/src/scheduling/cancelBooking.ts:43-44; apps/functions/src/scheduling/assignObservationFromPreference.ts:49-50; apps/functions/src/observations/geminiTagScript.ts:71-72; apps/functions/src/observations/backfillScriptTagColors.ts:39-40; apps/functions/src/email/sendManualEmail.ts:29-32; apps/functions/src/scheduling/createObservationWindow.ts:47-48; apps/functions/src/transcription/requestTranscription.ts:63-66; contrast apps/functions/src/observations/uploadEvidenceFile.ts:93 and storage.rules:11-16
implementationPlan: 1) Create apps/functions/src/lib/authz.ts with callerEmail(request), callerIsAdmin(request) (token.isAdmin === true, falling back to isAdminRole(token.role)), and callerHasSpecialAccess(request) (token.hasSpecialAccess === true, falling back to isSpecialRole/isAdminRole). 2) Replace the inline role-slug checks in the nine listed callables; give requestTranscription the same admin-or-observer policy as geminiTagScript. 3) Unit tests for authz.ts covering hasAdminAccess-only tokens, role-only legacy tokens, and missing claims. 4) Keep getAudio as-is (hasSpecialAccess already covers the flagged users) or align it with the helper for consistency.

currentState: The schema documents empty assignedComponentIds as 'no components active for this role-year' (packages/shared/src/schema/settings.ts:15-18), and the web editor renders 'No components are assigned' for it (ObservationEditorPage.tsx:160-170 produces zero domains; MyRubricPage.tsx:81-101 same). currentState: The admin UI exposes a Recipient selector (To: Staff / To: PE / To: Both / To: Admin) on every template (apps/web/src/admin/email-templates/EmailTemplatesPage.tsx:659-674) and the schema documents it (packages/shared/src/schema/emailTemplate.ts:28-35, 91-92). implementationPlan: 1) Add a resolveRecipients(template, { observedEmail, observerEmail, adminEmail }) helper in emailUtils.ts and have sendTemplatedEmail accept the participant emails (observed/observer) instead of a raw `to`; compute recipients from template.recipient with sensible fallbacks. 2) Update all call sites (onObservationWritten, finalizeObservation, onStaffWritten, bookObservationSlot/createDraftObservationForBooking, cancelBooking, createObservationWindow, expireObservationWindows) to pass both party emails. 3) For triggers where only one party exists (staff.created), hide or fix the selector in EmailTemplatesPage. 4) Unit tests for resolveRecipients covering all four recipient types and missing-email fallbacks.

title: Invitee invited at two buildings only ever receives the first building's invite/token
currentState: createObservationWindow dedupes invitees per email+building (apps/functions/src/scheduling/createObservationWindow.ts:79-81), so the same person can legitimately appear twice with two different inviteTokens/buildingIds. But the invite mail doc id is keyed on email only — `scheduling.windowInvite-${windowId}-${invitee.email}` (:245) — so the second invitee entry's sendEmail does a .set() onto the SAME /mail doc, which the Trigger Email extension ignores (it only sends on doc creation, per apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:27-35,43-44). The person never receives the second building's bookingLink/token, and since bookObservationSlot matches invitee by email+token and enforces slot.buildingId === invitee.buildingId (apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:232-251), they can never book at the second building.
expectedBehavior: Each invitee entry (email+building) gets its own invite email with its own booking link.
evidence: apps/functions/src/scheduling/createObservationWindow.ts:79-81 (dedupe key allows same email twice) vs :245 (mailDocId keyed on email only); apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:250-252 (building check makes the lost token unrecoverable)
implementationPlan: 1) Include buildingId in the mail doc id: `scheduling.windowInvite-${windowId}-${invitee.email}-${invitee.buildingId}`. 2) Key the sentEmails set on email+buildingId so inviteSentAt stamping is per invitee entry (invitees.map already iterates entries). 3) Unit test: two invitee entries for one email produce two distinct mail doc ids.

currentState: Finalization is one-way: no reopenObservation or regeneratePdf callable exists (apps/functions/src/index.ts:15-43 exports neither) and no UI offers it. title: Failed upload permanently loses the recording — no retry, no download fallback, no unload guard
currentState: When the POST to uploadAudio fails (school Wi-Fi drop, 413, function cold-start timeout), AudioRecorder sets phase='error' and shows the message, but the only affordance left is the Record button, and startRecording wipes chunksRef (AudioRecorder.tsx:117 `chunksRef.current = []`), destroying the captured audio. There is no 'Retry upload' button even though the blob bytes are still in memory (AudioRecorder.tsx:188-205), and no 'Download recording' escape hatch. There is also no beforeunload guard anywhere in apps/web/src — closing or refreshing the tab mid-recording or mid-upload silently discards a possibly 40-minute, unrepeatable classroom recording.
expectedBehavior: On upload failure the captured blob is retained and the UI offers 'Retry upload' and 'Download recording' (a(download) with the blob URL) so the audio is never unrecoverable. While phase is 'recording' or 'uploading', a beforeunload handler warns before tab close/refresh. Optionally retry automatically with backoff once before surfacing the error.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/AudioRecorder.tsx:117 (chunks wiped on next Record), :162-206 (error path keeps blob but offers no retry/download), :101-106 (unmount cleanup), Grep for 'beforeunload' in apps/web/src returns no matches
implementationPlan: 1) AudioRecorder: store the failed Blob in state; in the error PhaseStatus render 'Retry upload' (re-invokes uploadRecording with the stored blob) and 'Download' (object-URL anchor with a timestamped filename). 2) Add a useEffect registering beforeunload while phase==='recording'||'uploading'. 3) Keep the popover open when phase==='error' in AudioPopoverButton's click-outside logic so the recovery UI isn't hidden. 4) Component tests: failed fetch → retry button re-posts the same blob; download link present.

title: Default-bitrate recordings hit the 32 MiB upload cap around 30 minutes — full-length observations rejected
currentState: MediaRecorder is constructed with only a mimeType (AudioRecorder.tsx:122) — no audioBitsPerSecond — so browsers record opus at roughly 96-128 kbps, which crosses uploadAudio's MAX_AUDIO_BYTES = 32 MiB (uploadAudio.ts:29, also the Cloud Functions ingress ceiling) at roughly 30-45 minutes. A typical 45-60 minute classroom observation produces a blob the server rejects with 413 (uploadAudio.ts:92-95) only AFTER the user waited through the full upload; combined with the missing retry path the audio is then lost. The client never checks blob.size before posting, never warns about elapsed time during recording, and nothing in the UI communicates a maximum length.
expectedBehavior: Recordings are encoded for speech (e.g. audioBitsPerSecond: 32000, channelCount: 1 → ~2.2 hours under 32 MiB). The client validates blob.size against the shared cap before uploading and shows a clear, friendly error (with download fallback) instead of a raw 413. The recorder shows a soft warning as the projected size approaches the cap.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/AudioRecorder.tsx:122 (no audioBitsPerSecond/channelCount), apps/functions/src/audio/uploadAudio.ts:29 (MAX_AUDIO_BYTES=32MiB) and :92-95 (413 after full body received)
implementationPlan: 1) packages/shared/src/constants.ts: export MAX_AUDIO_UPLOAD_BYTES (and re-export via index if needed); rebuild @ops/shared. 2) AudioRecorder: pass {audioBitsPerSecond: 32000} and getUserMedia audio constraints {channelCount: 1}; pre-check blob.size against the shared constant with a human-readable message; surface a duration warning past e.g. 90 minutes. 3) uploadAudio: import the shared constant instead of its local literal. 4) Tests: unit test the size pre-check branch; verify constant parity between client and function.

title: Acknowledgement state is invisible everywhere except the staff member's own dashboard

PEs and admins cannot track who has signed off, which is the whole point of the ack step; it's also missing from the PDF and the master-log Sheet columns (onObservationWritten.ts:21-36).
expectedBehavior: Finalized observations show an 'Acknowledged on <date>' badge (or 'Awaiting acknowledgement') in the list page, staff person page, and editor header; the Sheet sync includes an Acknowledged column.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/ObservationsListPage.tsx:255-268; apps/web/src/observations/GlobalToolsBar.tsx:71-88; apps/web/src/routes/StaffPersonPage.tsx:413-511; apps/functions/src/observations/onObservationWritten.ts:21-36 (no ack column); grep 'acknowledg' shows only dashboard consumers
implementationPlan: Add an ack chip to StatusBadge call sites (list, person page cards, editor header) reading acknowledgedAt; extend HEADER_ROW/buildRow + MEANINGFUL_FIELDS in onObservationWritten with acknowledgedAt; optionally stamp it into the PDF when present. Tests for the badge logic.

title: Autosave is whole-draft last-writer-wins — two open sessions silently destroy each other's work
currentState: The editor's flush() writes the entire draft (observationData, componentNotes, scriptDoc, meeting notes, name/date) with setDoc merge on every debounce (ObservationEditorPage.tsx:222-237), and hydration happens once per observation (useHydratedDraft.ts:26-36) so remote changes are never merged in. The same observer with the editor open on an iPad and a laptop (a stated target platform — Playwright runs iPad viewports) will have whichever device saves last erase the other's edits with no warning, no conflict detection, and no version field.
expectedBehavior: Concurrent sessions either merge at field granularity, detect conflicts (lastModifiedAt precondition / version counter) and warn before overwriting, or visibly lock the observation to one active session.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/ObservationEditorPage.tsx:217-248 (whole-draft setDoc merge from draftRef); apps/web/src/hooks/useHydratedDraft.ts:26-36 (snapshots after first hydration ignored)
implementationPlan: Minimum viable: add an updateSeq field bumped on each flush; before writing, compare the live snapshot's updateSeq against the locally-known one and surface a 'changed elsewhere — reload?' banner instead of writing. Better: split flush into per-section writes (only dirty keys) to shrink the clobber surface. Tests simulating an external write between hydration and flush.

title: Transcripts are a dead end — no insertion into the observation script (UI copy even claims otherwise)
currentState: A completed transcript renders only inside the audio popover's <details> block (AudioRecorder.tsx:397-404). There is no 'Insert into script' action, no copy-to-clipboard, and ScriptEditor has no transcript integration (only link insertion, ScriptEditor.tsx:318,520). Worse, the recorder's own help text promises 'Transcription is requested separately and lands in the script when ready' (AudioRecorder.tsx:215-216) — which is false; it lands in observation.transcripts and never touches scriptDoc. To use the transcript as scripting evidence (the whole point of the rubric-tagging workflow), the evaluator must manually select text inside a cramped popover and paste it into the editor.
expectedBehavior: Each transcript offers 'Insert into script' (appends the transcript as paragraphs to the Tiptap scriptDoc, where it can then be auto-tagged via geminiTagScript) and 'Copy' actions. The help copy accurately describes the behavior.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/AudioRecorder.tsx:215-216 (misleading copy), :397-404 (display-only transcript), apps/web/src/observations/ScriptEditor.tsx:318,520 (no transcript-related insertion API)
implementationPlan: 1) AudioRecorder: per-transcript 'Copy' (navigator.clipboard) and 'Insert into script' buttons (Draft + canEdit only). 2) Wire an onInsertTranscript(text) callback up through AudioPopoverButton → ObservationEditorPage, which splits the transcript on line breaks into paragraph nodes and appends to draft.scriptDoc via the existing scheduleSave path (or exposes the ScriptEditor's editor instance via ref and uses insertContent). 3) Fix the header copy to say transcripts attach to the recording and can be inserted into the script. 4) Component test: insert callback receives transcript text; copy button writes clipboard.

title: Connecting Google Calendar after booking never creates the missed events
currentState: onObservationBooked fires once per observation write and short-circuits when gcalEventIds is already populated; when NEITHER party has a connected calendar at booking time it writes a calendar.eventSkipped audit entry and gives up forever. If one party was connected, the ids are written and the guard permanently blocks creating the other party's copy. connectGoogleCalendar stores tokens but performs no backfill for existing upcoming bookings. So 'connect calendar' only helps future bookings — counterintuitive for staff who connect right after seeing their confirmation email.
expectedBehavior: After a successful connect, upcoming scheduled observations involving that user that lack an event on their calendar get events created (backfill), or at minimum the booked state on BookingPage tells the user their event was skipped and offers a retry.
evidence: apps/functions/src/calendar/onObservationBooked.ts:50 (eventIdsEmpty guard blocks partial retries — eventIdsEmpty returns false if either id exists, lines 15-20), 82-94 (skip path when neither connected); apps/functions/src/calendar/auth/connectGoogleCalendar.ts:84-104 (stores tokens, no backfill)
implementationPlan: In connectGoogleCalendar (post-token-write), query /observations where status==Draft, scheduledStartAt >= now and (observerEmail==caller or observedEmail==caller), and for each doc missing the caller's gcalEventIds entry call createObservationEvent for just that party and merge the id back (add a per-party variant or pass a single client). Relax onObservationBooked's guard to per-party (create observer copy when gcalEventIds.observer missing AND observer connected, same for observed) while keeping idempotence via the per-party id check. Add composite indexes for the two queries. Tests for the per-party guard helper.

title: Bell-schedule changes under a booked slot only write an audit warning — observation, calendar events, and people are never updated
currentState: When an admin edits a building schedule, onBuildingScheduleWritten reconciles available/blocked slots but explicitly 'never modifies booked slots': if a booked slot's period time changed or the period vanished, it pushes a bookedWarnings entry into /auditLog (visible only to admins who happen to read the audit log) and does nothing else. The booking's slot doc, the draft observation's scheduledStartAt/scheduledEndAt, and both Google Calendar events keep the OLD time; neither the PE nor the staff member is notified. People will show up at the wrong time.
expectedBehavior: Booked slots whose period time changed get their times updated in place (slot + observation + calendar events via updateObservationEvent) with an email to both parties; vanished periods trigger a forced cancellation or an explicit PE-facing task to rebook, not a silent audit row.
evidence: apps/functions/src/scheduling/onBuildingScheduleWritten.ts:151-159 ('never modify booked' + period-time-changed warning), 200-217 (period-removed → warning only), 228-241 (auditLog write is the entire handling); apps/functions/src/calendar/lib/googleCalendar.ts:339-358 (updateObservationEvent available but unused)
implementationPlan: For 'period-time-changed': update the booked slot's startUTC/endUTC/startMinute, rewrite the matching peBusyIntervals entry on the window, update the observation's scheduledStartAt/scheduledEndAt/observationDate, patch both calendar events with updateObservationEvent, and send a reschedule-notice email to bookedBy + observer. For 'period-removed': call the shared cancel-booking helper (frees invitee to rebook) and email both parties. Surface remaining warnings on the window detail page rather than only the audit log. Tests for the reconcile branches.

title: Resource fileUrl exists in schema and renderer but there is no file upload — resources are external-link-only despite Drive being the app's blob store
currentState: moduleItem.fileUrl is defined (packages/shared/src/schema/moduleItem.ts:25) and the end-user renderer falls back to it (apps/web/src/modules/moduleSections.tsx:62 `r.linkUrl ?? r.fileUrl`), but the admin editor only offers a Link URL text input (apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleSectionEditor.tsx:186-195). There is no upload path: apps/functions/src/lib/drive.ts and the audio upload callable (apps/functions/src/audio/uploadAudio.ts) prove the Drive + DWD plumbing exists, yet nothing module-related uses it (no module functions exist at all under apps/functions/src). An admin wanting to attach a PDF/handbook must host it elsewhere and paste a URL.
expectedBehavior: Admin can upload a file for a resource item (stored in the district Drive via the existing service account), producing a fileUrl/driveFileRef that staff open from the module page; uploaded files are shared with domain-readable permissions or per-assignment.
evidence: packages/shared/src/schema/moduleItem.ts:25 (fileUrl never settable); apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleSectionEditor.tsx:186-195 (link-only UI); apps/web/src/modules/moduleSections.tsx:62 (renderer ready); apps/functions/src/lib/drive.ts (existing Drive helper, unused for modules)
implementationPlan: 1) New callable apps/functions/src/modules/uploadModuleFile.ts (admin-gated; accepts moduleId/itemId + bytes or resumable-session pattern mirroring uploadAudio.ts; writes to a Modules Drive folder; returns webViewLink + driveFileRef). 2) Export from apps/functions/src/index.ts. 3) ModuleSectionEditor: file picker + upload state on resource items; write fileUrl (and optionally a driveFile ref field — schema addition in moduleItem.ts if so). 4) Render a file icon distinct from external links in moduleSections.tsx. 5) Tests for the callable's auth gate.

title: Items have an order field but no reorder UI, and order values collide after deletions
currentState: moduleItem.order drives sorting in both the admin editor (apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleSectionEditor.tsx:49-52) and the staff page (apps/web/src/modules/moduleSections.tsx:10-15), but the only write is order = sectionItems.length at creation (ModuleSectionEditor.tsx:58-72). There are no move up/down controls (sections have them; items don't), and deleting an item then adding a new one produces duplicate order values (delete order-0 of three, next add gets order 2 — colliding with the existing order-2 item, leaving tie order dependent on snapshot doc order).
expectedBehavior: Admin can reorder resources/materials within a section (up/down buttons matching the section controls, or drag), the list renders in that exact order for staff, and order values stay dense/unique across add/delete.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModuleSectionEditor.tsx:58-72 (order only set at creation), 126-241 (no item move controls); apps/web/src/modules/moduleSections.tsx:10-15 (sorted by order)
implementationPlan: 1) Add ArrowUp/ArrowDown buttons per item row in ModuleSectionEditor; swap order values of the two affected items via a writeBatch. 2) On removeItem, renumber the remaining section items in the same batch (or normalize orders on every add). 3) Tie-break the sorts by createdAt or itemId for determinism. 4) Unit test for the renumbering helper.

title: Email delivery failures are invisible — audit log records 'sent' at enqueue time
currentState: sendEmail writes the /mail doc and immediately writes an AUDIT_ACTIONS.emailSent audit entry (apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:108-128) — i.e., 'sent' really means 'queued'. The Trigger Email extension records delivery state (delivery.state = SUCCESS/ERROR, attempts, error message) back onto each /mail doc, but nothing in the repo reads it (grep 'delivery' → zero matches outside node_modules), and clients are fully locked out of /mail (firestore.rules:327-333). A bad SMTP config, bounced address, or extension outage produces zero signal anywhere — admins believe staff were notified when they were not.
expectedBehavior: Delivery failures surface to admins: an audit entry (or admin email-log page) distinguishes queued/delivered/failed, and persistent failures are visible (or alerted to securityAdminEmail).
evidence: apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:115-128 (audit 'emailSent' written before any delivery happens); firestore.rules:327-333 (/mail unreadable by clients); no code reads delivery state (repo-wide grep)
implementationPlan: 1) Add AUDIT_ACTIONS.emailFailed to packages/shared/src/schema/auditLog.ts; rebuild @ops/shared. 2) New onDocumentWritten trigger apps/functions/src/email/onMailDelivered.ts on mail/{id}: when delivery.state transitions to ERROR, write an auditLog entry with the error, recipients, and subject; optionally notify securityAdminEmail. 3) Export from apps/functions/src/index.ts. 4) AuditLogPage picks the action up automatically. 5) Unit-test the state-transition detection as a pure helper (before PROCESSING/SUCCESS → after ERROR).

title: First sign-in without a staff record dead-ends silently; no admin visibility or request path
currentState: A valid @orono.k12.mn.us user who signs in before an admin creates their staff doc gets claims {role:null} from syncMyClaims (which silently proceeds when the staff doc is missing, syncMyClaims.ts:37-44), lands on /dashboard and sees 'No staff record found for your account.' (StaffDashboardPage.tsx:259-267; ProfilePage.tsx:227-235 similar). Nothing is logged to the audit trail, no one is notified (the securityAdminEmail setting at settings.ts:187 has no consumers anywhere), and the user has no in-app way to request provisioning. Admins only learn via hallway conversation.

evidence: apps/functions/src/auth/syncMyClaims.ts:37-46; apps/web/src/dashboard/StaffDashboardPage.tsx:259-267; apps/web/src/routes/ProfilePage.tsx:227-235; packages/shared/src/schema/settings.ts:187 (securityAdminEmail unused — repo-wide grep)

title: Renaming a building silently orphans every staff building assignment
currentState: staff.buildings stores building displayName strings (acknowledged as legacy in building.ts:12-16), but BuildingsPage lets admins freely edit displayName with no cascade and no warning (BuildingsPage.tsx save ~240-252). After a rename, every assigned staff member's building becomes '(unmapped)', MyStaffPage building scoping matches nothing (administrators see an empty caseload), ProfilePage 'My Administrators' building matching breaks, and window-invitee building joins drift. Deletion at least warns (line ~348-352); rename does not.
expectedBehavior: Renaming a building keeps all staff assignments intact — either staff reference buildings by stable buildingId, or the rename cascades a batch update across staff docs with a confirmation showing the affected count.
evidence: packages/shared/src/schema/building.ts:12-16; apps/web/src/admin/buildings/BuildingsPage.tsx:240-252 (rename without cascade), 348-352 (delete warns, rename doesn't); apps/web/src/routes/MyStaffPage.tsx:52-58 (name-string matching); apps/web/src/routes/ProfilePage.tsx:185-189
implementationPlan: Short-term: in BuildingsPage save(), when displayName changed, query staff where('buildings','array-contains', oldName), show 'This will update N staff records', and batch-rewrite their buildings arrays (bulkMergePerRow). Long-term (preferred): migrate staff.buildings to buildingId slugs — schema note, one-shot migration callable (pattern: migrateRolesToSlugs), and update all display sites (StaffDialog, StaffInlineEditors, directory/person/profile pages, MyStaffPage scoping) to resolve id→displayName. Tests for the rename cascade.

currentState: AuditLogPage only pages newest-first 50 at a time (AuditLogPage.tsx:31-76) — no filter by action, user, target, or date range, no text search, no CSV export, and the AdminDataView sort capability is not wired (no sort prop at AuditLogPage.tsx:131-138 despite AdminDataView.tsx:58-75 supporting it). With 365-day retention (settings.ts:169) finding 'who edited staff X last month' means clicking Load-50-more dozens of times.

title: Observed staff can read full Draft observation content (evaluator's in-progress notes), while the rules' own doc says Finalized-only
currentState: The /observations rule header states 'observed staff member, ONLY if status == Finalized' (firestore.rules:236-239), but the actual get/list rules (252-258) let the observed staff member read ALL their observations including Standard Drafts — and the rules tests assert this deliberately ('observed teacher CAN read a Draft Standard observation about them', tests/rules/observations.test.ts:54, 260). The dashboard intentionally relies on it (useActiveStandardObservation.ts:6-13 surfaces draft cards), and ObservationEditorPage renders observed staff a read-only editor of the draft (ObservationEditorPage.tsx:275-277), exposing the evaluator's live script, component scratch notes, and provisional proficiency selections before finalization. Write-side is correctly type-restricted (WP/IR answers only, rules:280-286) but read-side has no equivalent restriction. So either the comment is wrong or the exposure is.
expectedBehavior: A deliberate, documented policy. If observed staff should see only that a draft exists (for dashboard pre/post cards) but not the evaluator's working notes, drafts' sensitive fields must move out of read reach (Firestore has no field-level read security); if full-draft visibility is the intended product behavior, the rules comment and editor UX should say so explicitly.
evidence: firestore.rules:236-239 (comment: Finalized-only) vs firestore.rules:252-258 (rule: observed reads any status); tests/rules/observations.test.ts:54,260 (drafts readable asserted intentionally); apps/web/src/hooks/useActiveStandardObservation.ts:6-13 (dashboard depends on draft reads); apps/web/src/observations/ObservationEditorPage.tsx:275-277 (observed gets read-only draft editor)
implementationPlan: Doc-only fix: rewrite the /observations rule header (firestore.rules:236-250) to document the actual policy — observed staff intentionally read all their own observations including Drafts (loosened May 2026 for dashboard pre/obs/post cards), with write-side restricted to WP/IR answers and finalized-ack fields. Optionally add a one-line product note in DESIGN.md. Reopen draft-privacy (the Option A mirror) only if the district raises it as a product requirement.

title: Rate-limit settings (saves/min, uploads/hr, transcriptions/day) have no enforcement
currentState: All three appSettings.rateLimits knobs are editable in SettingsPage, but no code path enforces any of them: uploadAudio, requestTranscription, and the observation autosave path contain no counting or limiting logic, and grep for the field names across apps/functions returns nothing.
expectedBehavior: Per-user enforcement: uploadAudio rejects beyond audioUploadsPerHour, requestTranscription rejects beyond transcriptionRequestsPerDay (it already creates transcriptionJobs docs that can be counted), and observation saves are throttled client-side per observationSavesPerMinute with a server backstop. Trips optionally alert securityAdminEmail.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:257-319 (editors); packages/shared/src/schema/settings.ts:40-44 (schema); grep for observationSavesPerMinute|audioUploadsPerHour|transcriptionRequestsPerDay in apps/functions/src returns no matches; apps/functions/src/audio/uploadAudio.ts and apps/functions/src/transcription/requestTranscription.ts have no limit checks
implementationPlan: Functions: add lib/rateLimit.ts with a checkRateLimit(db, userEmail, key, max, windowMs) helper backed by a /rateLimitCounters collection (transactional increment with window reset); call it in uploadAudio (audioUploadsPerHour) and requestTranscription (transcriptionRequestsPerDay), loading limits from appSettings with schema defaults. Web: debounce/cap autosave writes using observationSavesPerMinute read from settings. Tests: unit tests for the counter helper.

title: Transcription pipeline ignores observation status — worker mutates finalized observations and diverges from the PDF
currentState: uploadAudio enforces Draft (uploadAudio.ts:114-117) but requestTranscription performs no status check (requestTranscription.ts:58-69) and the worker writes transcripts.{fileId} + lastModifiedAt to the observation unconditionally (onTranscriptionJobCreated.ts:115-118). Two consequences: (a) the observer of a finalized observation can invoke the callable directly and alter the finalized record after the fact; (b) legitimately racing jobs — finalize while a transcription is Running — produce a PDF without the transcript (finalizeObservation gathers no transcription state, finalizeObservation.ts:75-128) and then the worker silently mutates the now-locked doc, so the in-app record and the archived PDF disagree. The FinalizeDialog lists audio counts but never warns about in-flight transcriptions (ObservationEditorPage.tsx:891-900).
expectedBehavior: requestTranscription rejects non-Draft observations (failed-precondition). The worker re-reads observation status before writing and marks the job Failed('observation finalized during transcription') instead of mutating a finalized doc. The FinalizeDialog warns (or finalize blocks) when a Pending/Running transcription exists for the observation.
evidence: apps/functions/src/transcription/requestTranscription.ts:58-69 (no status guard), apps/functions/src/transcription/onTranscriptionJobCreated.ts:115-118 (unconditional obsRef.update), apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeObservation.ts:75-128 (no transcription-job check), apps/web/src/observations/ObservationEditorPage.tsx:891-900 (dialog silent on in-flight jobs)
implementationPlan: Steps 1-3 as written. Step 4: apps/functions has no emulator test harness — follow the existing finalizeClaim.test.ts pattern: extract the status-guard / in-flight-job-check logic into pure helpers (fake tx/snapshot stubs) and unit-test those with vitest, rather than emulator tests.

title: Finalize flow has no completeness validation or warning
currentState: The FinalizeDialog lists only observed/type/audio-count (ObservationEditorPage.tsx:880-900) and finalizeObservation performs no content validation (finalizeObservation.ts:50-233): an observation with zero scored components, empty notes, or (for WP/IR) zero staff answers finalizes silently, locks, generates a near-empty PDF, and emails the staff member. Optionally add a server-side warning return or failed-precondition for fully empty WP/IR. Unit test the completeness derivation.

title: Scheduling-created Draft observations also fire generic 'observation created' emails (double email)
currentState: onObservationWritten sends an observation.created.* email for EVERY newly created observation doc with an observedEmail (apps/functions/src/observations/onObservationWritten.ts:96-140) — there is no check for windowId/slotId. Bookings create Draft observations via createDraftObservationForBooking (apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:90-123), which already sends a bookingConfirmation/assignmentNotice (:158-170). So a booked staff member gets both 'Observation scheduled for …' and (if the matching creation template is active) 'A Standard Observation Has Been Started'. The standard creation template ships inactive (scripts/import/seed.ts:100), but the Work Product and Instructional Round creation templates ship ACTIVE (seed.ts:163, 180) — a window whose defaultObservationType is WP/IR double-emails every booking.
expectedBehavior: Observations spawned by the scheduling flow suppress the generic creation email; the booking confirmation is the single source of truth for that event.
evidence: apps/functions/src/observations/onObservationWritten.ts:96-105 (creation email with no windowId guard); apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:112-114 (booking-created docs carry windowId/slotId) and :158 (confirmation already sent); scripts/import/seed.ts:163,180 (WP/IR creation templates active by default)
implementationPlan: 1) In onObservationWritten, skip the creation email when afterData['windowId'] is set (booking flow owns the notification). 2) Unit test: a creation event with windowId set sends nothing; without windowId sends the right trigger per type.

title: requireCalendarConnect is bypassed by the day-preference flow and surfaces only as a server error in direct booking

title: Day-preference staff cannot withdraw a preference, and have no cancel button after assignment

Staff who become unavailable have no self-service path and the day's capacity stays consumed.
