implementationPlan: Shared: add withdrawDayPreferenceInput {windowId, inviteToken}. Functions: new withdrawDayPreference callable — transaction validates token + unassigned preference, deletes the preference doc, decrements dayCounts via applyDayCountChange-style logic; export from index.ts. Web: in BookingPage, render a 'Withdraw' button next to 'Update preference' for an existing unassigned pref, and reuse the existing cancel() handler (cancelBookingFn) in the assigned branch. Tests for dayCounts decrement.

title: No reminder email nudges invitees who haven't booked before the window closes
currentState: Invitees get exactly one invite email at window creation and then nothing until an after-the-fact 'window expired' notice. There is no scheduled nudge ('the window closes in N days and you haven't booked') even though the data needed (invitee.bookedSlotId, window.endDate, inviteSentAt) is all present, and EMAIL_TRIGGER_TYPES has no scheduling reminder trigger. The static invite mailDocId also makes any naive re-send a silent no-op.
expectedBehavior: A daily job (or extension of expireObservationWindows/scheduledEmailReminders) sends a 'scheduling.bookingReminder' templated email to non-booked invitees N days before endDate, with N configurable on the template (scheduledDays) and a per-day idempotent mailDocId.
evidence: packages/shared/src/schema/emailTemplate.ts:9-24 (no scheduling reminder trigger type); apps/functions/src/scheduling/expireObservationWindows.ts:24-31 (only post-expiry); apps/functions/src/scheduling/createObservationWindow.ts:245 (static mailDocId pattern that a reminder must NOT reuse)
implementationPlan: Shared: add 'scheduling.bookingReminder' to EMAIL_TRIGGER_TYPES; seed a system template. Functions: in the daily scheduler (new job or inside scheduledEmailReminders), query open/partially-booked windows where endDate is within template.scheduledDays of today, and for each invitee with bookedSlotId == null send the template with bookingLink, using mailDocId `scheduling.bookingReminder-{windowId}-{email}-{runDateYMD}`. Admin UI picks up the new trigger automatically via TRIGGER_LABELS (add label). Tests for the date-window selection helper.

title: Rules let the observer raw-write/delete window docs (ledger, tokens, dayCounts) and expose all invitees' tokens to every invitee
currentState: firestore.rules grants the observer unrestricted update/delete on their window doc — a buggy or malicious client could rewrite peBusyIntervals, dayCounts, invitees (including inviteTokens and bookedSlotId) or hard-delete the window, orphaning the slots/preferences subcollections and every linked draft observation, bypassing all the callable-enforced invariants. Separately, any invitee can read the full window doc, which includes every other invitee's inviteToken, email, role, year and booked status (tokens are not alone sufficient to book, but this is unnecessary exposure).
expectedBehavior: Client writes to /observationWindows are denied entirely (all mutations flow through callables — nothing in the web app writes the window doc directly), and invitee-facing reads do not expose other invitees' tokens (e.g., move tokens to a server-only subcollection or accept the booking page reading only its own invitee entry via a callable).
evidence: firestore.rules:184-185 (allow update, delete for observer with no field restrictions); firestore.rules:179-181 (invited staff read the whole doc; invitees[] carries every inviteToken per packages/shared/src/schema/observationWindow.ts:43-47); grep shows no client-side setDoc/updateDoc on observationWindows in apps/web/src (all mutations are callables)
implementationPlan: Rules: change /observationWindows to `allow update, delete: if false;` (or admin-only delete), keeping reads as-is for now; add rules tests asserting observer update/delete are denied. Token exposure (larger): mint tokens into a server-only /observationWindows/{id}/tokens subcollection keyed by email, drop inviteToken from invitees[], and have BookingPage rely on the URL token + server-side validation only — requires coordinated changes in createObservationWindow, bookObservationSlot, submitDayPreference, MyObservationWindowsPage copyLinks (move link-building to a callable or store own-token on the invitee doc). Phase 1 (deny writes) is low-risk and immediate.

title: Observer/Peer Evaluator names render as email localparts instead of admin-entered staff names
currentState: Observations denormalize observedName/Role/Year but not observerName (observation.ts:69-75). The teacher dashboard's Peer Evaluator card fabricates a name via observerEmail.split('@')[0] (StaffDashboardPage.tsx:269-276) — teachers cannot resolve it client-side because rules block them from reading other staff docs. title: Add-staff form accepts non-domain emails and silently overwrites existing records
currentState: StaffDialog's only email validation is `.includes('@')` (StaffDialog.tsx:191-194). An admin can create staff with a typo'd or non-@orono.k12.mn.us address — the invite email goes to the wrong address and the record can never match a sign-in (sign-in is domain-restricted), producing a ghost account. In create mode the save uses setDoc(..., {merge:true}) keyed on email (211-227), so 'adding' an email that already exists silently merges over the existing record (and re-stamps createdAt) with no duplicate warning. Nothing validates against the shared staffInput Zod schema before write, and rules don't validate shape either.
expectedBehavior: Create form enforces the @orono.k12.mn.us domain (or requires explicit override), validates via the shared staffInput schema, and detects an existing doc — offering 'open existing record' instead of silent overwrite.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/staff/StaffDialog.tsx:189-227; packages/shared/src/schema/staff.ts:36-55 (staffInput unused by the dialog); packages/shared/src/schema/common.ts (email schema unused at write time)
implementationPlan: 1) Validate the assembled doc with staffInput.safeParse before write; surface issues. 2) Enforce email.endsWith('@' + ALLOWED_EMAIL_DOMAIN) in create mode. 3) In create mode, getDoc the target id first; if exists, show 'Staff member already exists' with an Edit shortcut. 4) Add a small Vitest for the validation helper (extract to a pure function).

title: Staff invite email only fires on doc creation — no resend action, no invite on reactivation, and cutover import will mass-send
currentState: The invite email sends only when a staff doc is created (isNewStaff = !before.exists, onStaffWritten.ts:64-65). The emailUtils helper comment claims 're-inviting a staff member (e.g. ... a re-activation) creates a new /mail doc and actually re-sends' (emailUtils.ts:40-44), but no code path sends on isActive false→true, and there is no admin 'Resend invite' action anywhere on StaffPage. Conversely, the prod cutover import (scripts/import) creates hundreds of staff docs via Admin SDK, which will fire onStaffWritten and blast an invite email to every imported staff member unless the admin remembers to deactivate the 'staff.created' template first — the import README never mentions this.
expectedBehavior: Admins can resend an invite from the staff row menu; restoring an archived staff member optionally re-invites; the bulk importer cannot accidentally mass-email (suppression flag or documented procedure).
evidence: apps/functions/src/auth/onStaffWritten.ts:64-95; apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:37-48 (comment vs reality); apps/web/src/admin/staff/StaffPage.tsx:309-355 (row actions: no resend); scripts/import/README.md (no template-deactivation step)
implementationPlan: 1) New callable apps/functions/src/email/resendStaffInvite.ts (admin-only; reuses sendTemplatedEmail + staffInviteMailDocId) exported from index.ts. 2) StaffPage RowActions: add 'Resend invite email'. 3) Import safety: have the importer stamp importedAt on staff docs and skip the invite in onStaffWritten when present (or document deactivating the staff.created template in scripts/import/README.md). 4) Unit test for the skip condition.

title: My Staff caseload does not reflect role-year mappings (the configured 'who is evaluated' matrix)
currentState: Role-year mappings are documented as the lever for which role-years are actively evaluated ('Empty assignedComponentIds means no components active for this role-year — used to hide the rubric viewer entirely for staff in non-evaluation cycles', settings.ts:15-18). MyStaffPage derives the administrator caseload purely from staff.year>=4 || staff.summativeYear (MyStaffPage.tsx:60-70) and never reads /roleYearMappings. A probationary staff member whose role-year mapping is empty still shows as in-scope; a role-year the admin mapped components to (e.g. a specialist low-cycle year) never appears. Admin matrix edits therefore don't change any caseload view.
expectedBehavior: Caseload views agree with the admin-configured matrix: staff whose (role, year) has no assigned components are flagged or excluded, and the page makes the mapping linkage visible (e.g. an 'X components assigned' chip).
evidence: apps/web/src/routes/MyStaffPage.tsx:60-70; packages/shared/src/schema/settings.ts:15-18; apps/web/src/admin/role-year-mappings/RoleYearMappingsPage.tsx:122-150 (matrix writes that nothing downstream consumes for caseloads)
implementationPlan: 1) Add a shared helper (packages/shared) `hasAssignedComponents(mappings, roleId, year)`. 2) MyStaffPage: load /roleYearMappings (readable by all domain users per rules), join on roleYearMappingDocId(staff.role, staff.year), and render an 'Unmapped/No components' badge; optionally add a filter toggle 'Only mapped'. 3) Decide product behavior with stakeholder: exclude vs flag (default: flag, non-destructive). 4) Rebuild @ops/shared dist; add unit test for the helper.

title: Only the most recent draft WP/IR observation is answerable — a second active one dead-ends
currentState: useActiveWorkProductObservation / useActiveInstructionalRoundObservation use limit(1) on the newest draft (useActiveWorkProductObservation.ts:11-20), and MyRubricPage renders at most one answer form per type (MyRubricPage.tsx:157-159). If two evaluators (or one evaluator twice) create draft WP observations for the same teacher — nothing prevents this in CreateObservationDialog.tsx:58-98 — the older observation's questions are unreachable: the teacher can never see or answer them, yet the observer's viewer shows 'Not yet answered' indefinitely.
expectedBehavior: Every active WP/IR observation for the teacher is answerable (one form per active observation, labeled by observer/date), or duplicate active observations of the same type are prevented/warned at creation.
evidence: apps/web/src/hooks/useActiveWorkProductObservation.ts:11-20; apps/web/src/hooks/useActiveInstructionalRoundObservation.ts (same pattern); apps/web/src/routes/MyRubricPage.tsx:157-159; apps/web/src/observations/CreateObservationDialog.tsx:58-98
implementationPlan: Drop limit(1) (cap at a sane limit like 5) and return arrays from the hooks; ActiveObservationTypesContext exposes lists plus the existing first-item convenience; MyRubricPage maps over all active WP/IR observations rendering one form each with an observer/date header. Additionally, in CreateObservationDialog warn when the staff member already has a draft observation of the chosen type (query count). Existing composite index (observedEmail+type+status+createdAt) already supports the unbounded query.

title: Role's rubricId is free text with no existence validation — a typo silently breaks the whole rubric chain for that role
currentState: The role dialog's Rubric ID field is a plain Input (RolesPage.tsx:322-332) saved without checking that /rubrics/{rubricId} exists (RolesPage.tsx:250-263). title: Deleting a role breaks rubric resolution for its staff and all historical observations, with no in-use guard
currentState: RolesPage hard-deletes the role doc after a text-only confirmation that admits 'staff currently assigned to this role will keep the role string' (RolesPage.tsx:272-284,393-398). Every lookup in the subsystem resolves rubrics through the role doc: MyRubricPage (MyRubricPage.tsx:63-73), the observation editor (ObservationEditorPage.tsx:111-116), the sidebar domain nav (AppSidebar.tsx:289-294), and server-side finalize (roleLookup.ts → finalizeObservation.ts:85-91). After deletion, staff in that role lose their rubric page, finalized observations of that role render an error instead of their scored rubric, and any straggler draft can never be finalized. No check counts staff or observations referencing the roleId before allowing deletion.

evidence: apps/web/src/admin/roles/RolesPage.tsx:272-284,393-398; apps/web/src/observations/ObservationEditorPage.tsx:111-116; apps/web/src/routes/MyRubricPage.tsx:63-73

Also consider keeping finalized-observation rendering resilient by reading the rubric from the observation snapshot once the rubric-snapshot gap lands.

title: Question bank shows a drag handle but has no reordering at all, and order numbers can collide
currentState: Each question row renders a cursor-grab GripVertical handle that does nothing (WorkProductPage.tsx:153-155), and the page footer admits 'Reordering via drag-and-drop will land in Phase 7' (WorkProductPage.tsx:199-202). There are no up/down buttons either, and order is assigned as sorted.length at creation (WorkProductPage.tsx:42), so after deleting a question a new one can duplicate an existing order value, making display order ambiguous. title: Module page rich-text content is readable by every signed-in staff member, not just assigned staff
currentState: The assignment gate only protects the items subcollection (firestore.rules:137-143). The module doc itself — including the full sections array with every rich-text body — is readable by anyone in the domain (firestore.rules:118-121), because chips/nav need displayName/color/icon for all modules. The ModulePage UI gate (apps/web/src/modules/ModulePage.tsx:83) is cosmetic; any staff member can read the raw doc (network tab/REST) and see cohort-targeted content, e.g. probationary-only or summative-only guidance written in richtext sections.

title: Creating a module with an existing ID silently merges into (and partially overwrites) the existing module
currentState: The create dialog's save uses setDoc(..., { merge: true }) keyed on the user-editable slug with no existence check (apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModulesPage.tsx:296-310). In create mode it also spreads a fresh createdAt. Typing a slug that collides with an existing module (easy: auto-slug from a similar display name) silently renames/recolors the existing module, resets its createdAt and autoEnable, while keeping its sections/items — a confusing partial clobber with no warning.
expectedBehavior: Create fails fast with 'A module with this ID already exists' (and offers to open it); IDs are only ever claimed once.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/modules/ModulesPage.tsx:289-310 (no uniqueness check; merge:true in create mode)
implementationPlan: 1) In save() for mode==='create', getDoc the target ref first (or use a transaction asserting !snap.exists()) and set a field error on collision. 2) Use setDoc without merge for create so the doc shape is exactly the form. 3) The loaded modules list is already available — also pre-validate against it for instant feedback. 4) Component test for the collision path.

title: Non-Orono Google accounts are signed out silently with no explanation, and the orphan Auth user is left behind
currentState: There is no Auth blocking function (Spark plan — index.ts:9-10 documents its removal; the firestore.rules header at lines 7-8 still references the deleted functions/src/auth/beforeCreate.ts). A user picking a personal Gmail in the popup completes Firebase sign-in (the `hd` param is only a chooser hint, SignInScreen.tsx:36), a Firebase Auth user record is created, then AuthProvider silently signs them out (AuthProvider.tsx:62-65) — `setError` in SignInScreen is never reached because signInWithPopup resolved successfully, so the user lands back on the sign-in screen with zero feedback. The orphaned non-domain Auth account persists in the project forever.
expectedBehavior: A rejected non-domain sign-in shows a clear message ('Sign-in is restricted to @orono.k12.mn.us accounts — you signed in as x@gmail.com') and the just-created stray Auth user is deleted.
evidence: apps/web/src/auth/AuthProvider.tsx:62-65 (silent signOut, no user-visible state); apps/web/src/auth/SignInScreen.tsx:31-55 (error only set on popup rejection); apps/functions/src/index.ts:9-10 (blocking function removed); firestore.rules:7-8 (stale beforeCreate.ts reference)
implementationPlan: 1) SignInScreen.signIn: after `signInWithPopup` resolves, check `result.user.email` against ALLOWED_EMAIL_DOMAIN; if it fails, call `result.user.delete()` (allowed for a freshly signed-in user; fall back to signOut if delete throws), then set a persistent error message naming the rejected account. 2) AuthProvider: when the domain check kicks a user out, record the rejected email in module state or sessionStorage so SignInScreen can render the explanation on the redirect-back path too. 3) Update the firestore.rules header comment to describe the actual layering (hd hint -> client check -> rules domain check -> syncMyClaims refusal) instead of the deleted beforeCreate.ts. 4) Component test for the rejection message.

title: Role / admin-access / activation changes never reach the audit log
currentState: The app has an /auditLog collection, an admin Audit Log page (apps/web/src/admin/audit-log/AuditLogPage.tsx), and functions write audit entries for emails and scheduling actions (apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:115-124, plus 14 function files referencing auditLog). But permission changes — the most security-relevant admin action — are direct client writes to /staff (StaffPage.tsx onPatch, StaffInlineEditors.tsx:202 toggling hasAdminAccess, BulkEditDialog.tsx:198-207 bulk hasAdminAccess/isActive) and the claim-sync trigger only logger.info()s (onStaffWritten.ts:61). No record of who promoted/demoted/archived whom exists anywhere an admin can see.
expectedBehavior: Every change to role, hasAdminAccess, or isActive produces an auditLog entry (before/after values, affected email, timestamp; actor where derivable) visible on the admin Audit Log page.
evidence: apps/functions/src/auth/onStaffWritten.ts:60-61 (logger only, no auditLog write); apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:115-124 (the established auditLog write pattern this should reuse); apps/web/src/admin/staff/StaffInlineEditors.tsx:202 and apps/web/src/admin/staff/BulkEditDialog.tsx:198-207 (unaudited permission writes)
implementationPlan: 1) In onStaffWritten, diff before/after on role, hasAdminAccess, isActive; when any changed, add an auditLog doc { type: 'staff.permissions-changed', email, changes: {field: {from, to}}, at: serverTimestamp() }. Note the Firestore trigger cannot see the acting admin; either accept that or have the admin UI route permission edits through a small callable (updateStaffPermissions) that records callerEmail and writes the staff doc server-side. 2) Extract the auditLog write into a shared helper (apps/functions/src/lib/audit.ts) reused by emailUtils. 3) Add the new entry type's rendering/filters to AuditLogPage. 4) Unit test the diff logic.

title: Staff dashboard drops every listener error and renders a plausible-but-wrong empty state
currentState: StaffDashboardPage destructures only data from all eight Firestore hooks (StaffDashboardPage.tsx:69-139) and ignores the moduleMaterials useQuery error (lines 102-105), even though useFirestoreDoc/useFirestoreCollection expose error (useFirestoreCollection.ts:76-79). If the observations or windows listener fails (missing index, rules change, offline), tasks compute to [] and the hero says 'No active checkpoints right now' (DashboardView.tsx:317-321) — indistinguishable from a legitimately empty cycle.

title: Slot generation/booking never consults the evaluator's actual Google Calendar availability
currentState: The only conflict source the engine knows is in-app bookings (peBusyIntervals). The PE's real calendar — meetings, PTO, other district events — is never consulted, even though the PE may have connected Google Calendar (write-only calendar.events scope is requested, so freebusy cannot even be queried today). Staff can book a slot the evaluator visibly cannot attend, and the PE only finds out when the event lands on their calendar.
expectedBehavior: Optionally (admin/window toggle), slot generation or the booking check queries the observer's freebusy for the window range and blocks/grays conflicting slots, refreshed periodically or at booking time.
evidence: apps/functions/src/calendar/lib/googleCalendar.ts:31 (only calendar.events scope — no freebusy/readonly); apps/functions/src/scheduling/engine/slotGeneration.ts:110-168 and bookObservationSlot.ts:259 (conflict inputs are bell schedule + peBusyIntervals only); apps/web/src/scheduling/connectCalendar.ts:17 (client requests the same single scope)
implementationPlan: Add 'https://www.googleapis.com/auth/calendar.freebusy' (or calendar.readonly) to CALENDAR_OAUTH_SCOPE list on web + functions scope check; on createObservationWindow and in recomputeBlockedSlots, if the observer has a connected calendar, call freebusy.query for the window range and mark conflicting slots blocked with a new 'observer-busy' blockedReason (extend slotBlockedReason enum); add a schedulingSettings toggle `checkObserverCalendar`. Existing connections lack the new scope — handle gracefully (skip + surface 'reconnect to enable availability sync' on the profile page). Tests for the freebusy→blocked mapping.

title: Security admin email is collected but no security alert ever sends
currentState: appSettings.securityAdminEmail is a required schema field and editable in SettingsPage ('Receives security alerts (rate-limit trips, sign-in rejections, etc.)'), but no function reads it and no security-alert pipeline exists. Web: no change. Test: emailUtils test for admin-recipient resolution.

title: Session duration setting never enforced
currentState: appSettings.sessionDurationHours is editable ('How long a sign-in session is honored before the user is asked to re-authenticate') but nothing reads it. Firebase Auth sessions persist indefinitely via refresh tokens; AuthProvider performs no auth_time/session-age check, and no function or rule consults the setting.
expectedBehavior: Sessions older than the configured duration force re-authentication: AuthProvider compares the ID token's auth_time against sessionDurationHours and signs the user out (or forces reauth) when exceeded; optionally rules also compare request.auth.token.auth_time for defense in depth.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/settings/SettingsPage.tsx:129-141 (editor + claim); packages/shared/src/schema/settings.ts:168 (schema); apps/web/src/auth/AuthProvider.tsx:56-145 (no session-age logic); only other references are seed data (scripts/import/seed.ts:377) and a rules write test (tests/rules/admin-collections.test.ts:152)
implementationPlan: Web: in AuthProvider's onIdTokenChanged handler, read sessionDurationHours from /appSettings/global (one-shot getDoc), compare Date.now() - authTime from getIdTokenResult().authTime, and call signOut() with a 'session expired' notice when exceeded; re-check on an interval while the tab is open. Optionally add an auth_time guard helper in firestore.rules using a hardcoded ceiling. Tests: AuthProvider unit test with mocked token result.

title: Required/typed signup-field answers are not validated server-side
currentState: Required signup fields are enforced only client-side (signupFieldsComplete). bookObservationSlot accepts any detailAnswers (no required check, no membership check, no value validation); submitDayPreference checks membership only. Admins can also configure an active+required 'select' field with zero options, which makes the booking form impossible to complete.
expectedBehavior: Both callables load the window's selected fields, verify every required field has a non-empty answer, verify select values are in options and before-after values are one of the two constants, and reject malformed payloads. title: Building schedule 'Schedule active' toggle is never consumed
currentState: BuildingSchedulePage exposes an isActive checkbox ('Schedule active') and the schema carries it, but no consumer checks it: generateSlotsForWindow generates slots from inactive schedules, createObservationWindow loads the schedule doc without checking isActive, and onBuildingScheduleWritten regenerates slots regardless. Deactivating a schedule changes nothing.
expectedBehavior: An inactive schedule behaves like a missing schedule: createObservationWindow reports it in missingSchedules (failed-precondition), slot generation skips it, and the schedule-change trigger blocks its future slots — or the toggle is removed if effectiveFrom/effectiveTo make it redundant.

implementationPlan: Functions: in createObservationWindow treat !schedule.isActive as a missing schedule; in generateSlotsForWindow skip inactive schedules (defensive); in onBuildingScheduleWritten, when the after-doc is inactive, run the reconcile with an empty desired set so future available slots flip to blocked. Tests: extend slotGeneration.test.ts with an inactive-schedule case.

title: createObservationWindow doesn't enforce allowedBookingModes, past dates, or signupFieldIds validity server-side
currentState: The admin's allowedBookingModes setting is honored only by the create dialog's <select> filter; the callable accepts any bookingMode regardless (no settings read happens before validation — scheduling settings are only loaded later for the invite-email toggle). The callable also accepts a window entirely in the past (slots generate, then the window expires the next 07:30 run after sending invites for unbookable dates) and accepts arbitrary signupFieldIds strings without checking they reference existing/active fields applicable to the mode.
expectedBehavior: The server is the policy authority: reject bookingMode not in scheduling.allowedBookingModes, reject endDate before today (Chicago), and reject signupFieldIds that don't match active applicable /signupFields docs.
evidence: apps/functions/src/scheduling/createObservationWindow.ts:53-64 (validation block — only date-order and minute-order checks; settings not loaded until line 201, after all writes); apps/web/src/observations/CreateObservationWindowDialog.tsx:355 (client-only mode filtering); packages/shared/src/schema/settings.ts:145 (allowedBookingModes policy)
implementationPlan: Move the loadSchedulingSettings call to the top of createObservationWindow; add guards: bookingMode ∈ scheduling.allowedBookingModes; endDate >= chicagoDateString(new Date()) (reuse helper from bookingRules); fetch the referenced signupFields docs and reject unknown/inactive/inapplicable ids. Mirror the endDate>=today constraint in the dialog (min attribute on the date inputs). Unit tests for the guards.

currentState: Three drifts between what functions send and what the admin editor offers: (1) staffYear is populated by onStaffWritten.ts:86 and onRoleYearMappingWritten.ts:58 but is absent from KNOWN_TEMPLATE_VARIABLES (packages/shared/src/schema/emailTemplate.ts:41-77), so it has no insert chip, no label, and no preview sample — and the variables array (z.enum of known vars) would reject it. title: Multiple active templates per trigger silently pick an arbitrary winner; system-template integrity is UI-only
currentState: loadActiveTemplate runs where(triggerType).where(isActive).limit(1) with no orderBy (apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:54-68) — if two active templates share a trigger, which one sends is indeterminate. The admin UI freely allows this: any template's trigger can be changed to any value, including re-pointing a system template (EmailTemplatesPage.tsx:644-657), creating a second active template for a trigger with no warning or uniqueness check. Protection of system templates is UI-only (delete button hidden, EmailTemplatesPage.tsx:723-733) — firestore.rules:167-169 allow any admin write/delete, so a direct delete of e.g. the finalization template kills that notification with no trace.
expectedBehavior: The admin UI warns about (or prevents) two active templates on the same automatic trigger; rules prevent deleting isSystem templates; the loader is deterministic.
evidence: apps/functions/src/lib/emailUtils.ts:58-63 (limit(1), no orderBy); apps/web/src/admin/email-templates/EmailTemplatesPage.tsx:644-657 (unrestricted trigger reassignment) and :285-291 (toggleActive with no duplicate check); firestore.rules:167-169 (no isSystem delete guard)
implementationPlan: 1) Web: in toggleActive/saveTemplate, check loaded templates for another active template with the same non-manual trigger and show a confirm/warning (or auto-deactivate the other). 2) Rules: `allow delete: if isAdmin() && resource.data.isSystem != true;` for /emailTemplates; add a rules test. 3) Functions: add orderBy('updatedAt','desc') (or document the uniqueness invariant) in loadActiveTemplate so behavior is deterministic; requires a composite index entry in firestore.indexes.json. 4) Update tests/rules/admin-collections.test.ts.

currentState: Server-side resolveRole falls back to a displayName equality match for un-migrated observations whose observedRole holds the role's display name (apps/functions/src/observations/roleLookup.ts:14-33), 'so finalization keeps working for un-migrated docs'. title: Failed finalize leaves an orphan PDF in Drive and the folder shared with the observed staff member while the doc reverts to Draft
currentState: The Drive phase of finalize is upload → share → getDriveLinks (apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeObservation.ts:130-161). If share or getDriveLinks throws (or the subsequent obsRef.update at lines 165-169 fails), the catch rolls the observation back to Draft (lines 218-231) but performs no Drive cleanup: the uploaded PDF stays in the folder (a retry uploads a second file with the same name — Drive allows duplicates, so the folder accumulates near-identical PDFs and the observed staff member can't tell which is authoritative), and if shareWithUser succeeded before a later step failed, the observed staff member retains Reader on the folder of what is now a Draft — they can browse draft-state PDFs, audio, and evidence they should not yet see. Contrast uploadEvidenceFile, which deletes its orphan on failure (apps/functions/src/observations/uploadEvidenceFile.ts:151-163).
expectedBehavior: A failed finalize leaves no residue: the uploaded PDF is best-effort deleted in the failure path, and any share granted during the failed attempt is revoked, so the Draft's folder is exactly as private as before the attempt.
evidence: apps/functions/src/observations/finalizeObservation.ts:130-161 (no cleanup inside the Drive catch), 218-231 (rollback only resets Firestore status); apps/functions/src/observations/uploadEvidenceFile.ts:151-163 (the orphan-cleanup pattern that finalize lacks); apps/functions/src/lib/drive.ts:95-136 (shareWithUser has no revoke counterpart)
implementationPlan: Track pdfFileId/shared flags in the Drive try block; in its catch, best-effort drive.files.delete(pdfFileId) and, if the share was created in this attempt, remove the observed user's permission (add a revokeUserPermission helper to lib/drive.ts using permissions.list + permissions.delete). Alternatively/additionally, make the upload idempotent: before uploading, list the folder for an existing file with the same name and replace via files.update instead of create. Unit-test the cleanup ordering with mocked drive helpers.

title: Question bank edits write to Firestore on every keystroke with no error handling and no delete confirmation
currentState: Inline text editing calls setDoc per keystroke with no debounce (WorkProductPage.tsx:159-162 onChange → update), update() and destroy() have no try/catch and their promises are voided, so a rules rejection or network failure is silently swallowed — the admin sees their typing 'stick' locally while the doc is stale (WorkProductPage.tsx:61-74). Delete is a single click on an X icon with zero confirmation (WorkProductPage.tsx:164-171), permanently removing a question that may have answers across observations.
expectedBehavior: Debounced autosave with a visible saving/saved/error indicator (matching the rubric editor pattern), and a confirm step before destructive deletion.
evidence: apps/web/src/admin/work-product/WorkProductPage.tsx:61-74,159-171
implementationPlan: Add a local draft map + 500ms debounce for text edits (mirror WorkProductAnswerForm's pattern), a per-row or page-level save-state indicator, try/catch with an error banner for update/destroy, and an inline confirm (like RubricGridEditor's delete-confirmation row) before deleteDoc. title: Rubric editor saves without validating against the shared schema

title: Audio playback requires a manual full download — no streaming, Range support, or metadata
currentState: Playback is a two-step chore: the user must click 'Load audio', then the client fetches the ENTIRE file through getAudio into a Blob before an <audio> element appears (AudioRecorder.tsx:423-453). getAudio itself buffers the whole Drive file in function memory and responds without Content-Length or Accept-Ranges (getAudio.ts:96-102), so progressive playback/seeking-before-complete is impossible. For a 25-30 MiB recording on iPad Wi-Fi this is a long opaque spinner, and recordings are labeled only 'Recording 1/2/3' with no date or duration even though the Drive filename encodes the timestamp (uploadAudio.ts:127).
expectedBehavior: Recordings render an audio player immediately; bytes stream (function pipes the Drive stream with Content-Length, supports Range or at least progressive download), and each row shows the recording's date/time pulled from the filename or stored metadata.
evidence: apps/web/src/observations/AudioRecorder.tsx:423-453 ('Load audio' button, full-blob fetch), apps/functions/src/audio/getAudio.ts:96-102 (buffered send, no Range/Content-Length), apps/functions/src/audio/uploadAudio.ts:127 (timestamped filename never surfaced)
implementationPlan: As written, minus the Content-Length framing: Express already sets Content-Length on buffered sends. The real work is (1) piping Drive's stream instead of buffering, with Range support for Safari; (2) auto-load with progress (or signed-URL <audio src>); (3) date labels from the filename or stored metadata.

title: Demoting or archiving a privileged user leaves their old admin/PE token valid for up to an hour
currentState: onStaffWritten updates custom claims (onStaffWritten.ts:60) but never calls revokeRefreshTokens; its own docstring acknowledges 'existing tokens still carry old claims until they refresh' (lines 24-26). An admin who removes someone's access has no way to make it effective immediately — the demoted user keeps isAdmin/hasSpecialAccess in their live ID token for up to ~60 minutes, passing all firestore.rules checks and callable claim checks for that window.
expectedBehavior: Privilege downgrades (special/admin -> lower) take effect promptly: refresh tokens are revoked so the next SDK token refresh (minutes, not an hour) drops the old claims; upgrades can keep the lazy path.
evidence: apps/functions/src/auth/onStaffWritten.ts:24-26 (documented stale-token window) and :60 (setCustomUserClaims with no revokeRefreshTokens)
implementationPlan: 1) In onStaffWritten, compute before/after privilege levels; when hasSpecialAccess or isAdmin transitions true->false (including the isActive-based revocation from the deactivation fix), call getAuth().revokeRefreshTokens(user.uid) after setCustomUserClaims. 2) Note the client impact: the revoked user's current ID token still works until expiry (max 1h) for rules, but verifyIdToken(token, true) checks in HTTP functions would reject — document that rules-level immediacy requires checkRevoked, which Firestore rules cannot do, so the practical win is faster claim refresh and invalidated refresh tokens. 3) Unit test the transition detection.

title: Domain enforcement never checks email_verified — rules and claim-minting trust any token whose email string matches
currentState: isFromOronoDomain() (firestore.rules:32-36) and syncMyClaims (syncMyClaims.ts:29-35) match only the email string, not request.auth.token.email_verified. Today only Google SSO is enabled and Google emails are verified, so this is latent — but if email/password or another provider were ever enabled in the Firebase console, an attacker could register an unverified user with an @orono.k12.mn.us address and pass the domain gate, then mint claims for any pre-provisioned staff email via syncMyClaims. The rules-test harness already has to set email_verified:true for the emulator (tests/rules/harness.ts:24-30), showing the suite would support the stricter check.
expectedBehavior: Defense-in-depth: isFromOronoDomain() also requires `request.auth.token.email_verified == true`, and syncMyClaims refuses unverified emails — making the domain restriction robust to future provider configuration changes.
evidence: firestore.rules:32-36 (no email_verified condition); apps/functions/src/auth/syncMyClaims.ts:29-35 (no email_verified check); tests/rules/harness.ts:24-30 (harness already models verified emails)
implementationPlan: 1) firestore.rules: add `&& request.auth.token.email_verified == true` to isFromOronoDomain(); refresh the stale layered-defense header comment (beforeCreate.ts reference) while in the file. 2) syncMyClaims: reject when request.auth.token.email_verified !== true. 3) tests/rules/harness.ts: add an `unverified` claims helper; add tests asserting an unverified @orono token is denied reads. 4) Verify the dev-auth-server flow still works (it creates users with emailVerified:true, scripts/dev-auth-server.mjs:90).

title: Every render fetches Google Fonts over the network; image carries an unused firebase-admin dependency; undefined CSS variable

expectedBehavior: Fonts are baked into the image (self-hosted woff2 + @font-face, or apt fonts package) so rendering is hermetic and fast; no dead dependencies in the runtime image; all CSS variables used by the stylesheet are defined.
evidence: apps/pdf-renderer/src/template.ts:226 (@import google fonts), apps/pdf-renderer/src/index.ts:35 (networkidle0 dependency on the fetch); apps/pdf-renderer/Dockerfile:43-49 (no Lexend/Roboto installed); apps/pdf-renderer/package.json:26 (firebase-admin unused — no firebase import in src); apps/pdf-renderer/src/template.ts:338 (--ops-gray-light undefined, :root defines only --ops-gray/-dark/-lighter/-lightest at 227-239)
implementationPlan: Vendor Lexend + Roboto woff2 files into apps/pdf-renderer (assets/ copied into the image via Dockerfile) and replace the @import with @font-face declarations pointing at file:// or embedded base64; relax waitUntil to 'load' or 'domcontentloaded' once no network fetches remain. Remove firebase-admin from package.json (pnpm install to update lockfile). Fix `.empty` to use the defined --ops-gray. Verify with the template tests and a local docker:run render.

title: transcriptionJobs documents accumulate forever with no retention or cascade on observation delete

title: Catch-all 404 renders outside the auth shell: unauthenticated visitors see 'Page not found' instead of sign-in, signed-in users lose the app chrome
currentState: The `path="*"` route mounts NotFound directly (App.tsx:134), outside every StandardShell. An unauthenticated user hitting any mistyped/stale deep link gets a bare 'Page not found' card with a 'Back to home' link (NotFound.tsx) instead of being routed to /sign-in with their destination preserved (the behavior RequireAuth provides for known routes, RequireAuth.tsx:34-36). A signed-in user who typos a URL drops out of the Layout (no sidebar/header), which reads as a broken app state rather than a 404 inside the product.
expectedBehavior: Unknown paths behave like every other protected route: unauthenticated users are redirected to sign-in; authenticated users see the 404 inside the normal Layout with working navigation.
evidence: apps/web/src/App.tsx:134 (catch-all outside the shells); apps/web/src/routes/NotFound.tsx:4-16 (chrome-less render); apps/web/src/auth/RequireAuth.tsx:34-36 (the redirect-to-sign-in behavior unknown routes miss)
implementationPlan: 1) Move the catch-all inside the base StandardShell: add `<Route path="*" element={<NotFound />} />` as the last child of the no-special-access shell group and remove the top-level one (keep a public catch-all only if a pre-auth 404 is explicitly desired for /sign-in-adjacent paths). 2) Verify Suspense/Layout interaction (NotFound is not lazy, so no chunk concerns). 3) Add a Playwright assertion: unauthenticated /nonexistent redirects to /sign-in; authenticated /nonexistent shows 404 with sidebar.

title: Sidebar 'My Rubric' domain links go nowhere when the domain has no assigned components

expectedBehavior: Sidebar domain links either reflect only domains visible in the current mode, or navigating to a hidden domain's anchor switches the page to Full Rubric so the target exists.
evidence: apps/web/src/components/AppSidebar.tsx:289-299; apps/web/src/routes/MyRubricPage.tsx:86-101,115-122

title: Daily reminder job's queries have no composite indexes — pre-observation and incomplete-WP/IR reminders can never send in production
currentState: scheduledEmailReminders runs two Firestore queries that both require composite indexes: status=='Draft' + observationDate range (apps/functions/src/email/scheduledEmailReminders.ts:100-105) and status=='Draft' + type-in + createdAt range (:165-170). firestore.indexes.json declares neither (its observations indexes are status+lastModifiedAt, observerEmail+*, observedEmail+*). In production the first query throws FAILED_PRECONDITION; the await is not wrapped, so the whole scheduled function aborts — the incomplete-WP/IR phase never runs either. The emulator does not enforce indexes, so nothing in dev or tests catches this.
expectedBehavior: The daily 07:00 job runs both reminder phases successfully: pre-observation reminders N days ahead and incomplete WP/IR nudges N days after creation. A failure in one phase is isolated and logged, not fatal to the other.
evidence: apps/functions/src/email/scheduledEmailReminders.ts:100-105 and 165-170 (composite-index-requiring queries, bare awaits); firestore.indexes.json:12-62 (no status+observationDate index, no status+type+createdAt index for observations)
implementationPlan: 1) Add two composite indexes to firestore.indexes.json: observations(status ASC, observationDate ASC) and observations(status ASC, type ASC, createdAt ASC). 2) Wrap each reminder phase in its own try/catch in scheduledEmailReminders.ts so one phase failing (index, template, transient error) logs and continues instead of aborting the job. 3) Add a unit test asserting phase isolation (mock the first query to throw, assert the second still runs).

title: All PDFs/audio/evidence live in the service account's fixed ~15 GB My Drive — uploads and finalization will hard-fail once it fills, and no Shared Drive support exists (supportsAllDrives never set)
currentState: apps/functions/src/lib/drive.ts:5-12 documents that the Cloud Functions service account owns all observation folders directly and that 'Domain-Wide Delegation is OFF the table'. Every files.create/get/delete and permissions.create call omits supportsAllDrives, so the parent folder cannot be on a Shared Drive — all blobs are SA-owned My Drive files counting against the service account's fixed ~15 GB quota with no way to purchase more. A school year of 30-minute audio recordings (~30 MB each), evidence files (20 MB cap each), and PDFs across the district will exhaust it, at which point every audio upload, evidence upload, and finalize (PDF upload) returns Drive quota errors district-wide with no warning. README.md:13 and CLAUDE.md:21 still claim 'service account + Domain-Wide Delegation', contradicting the code.
expectedBehavior: Blob storage has effectively unbounded (Workspace-pooled) capacity: the parent folder lives on a Google Workspace Shared Drive, all Drive API calls pass supportsAllDrives/includeItemsFromAllDrives, and an automated check alerts the security admin before capacity or permission problems bite. Docs describe the real architecture.
evidence: apps/functions/src/lib/drive.ts:5-12 (SA-owned, DWD abandoned), entire file has zero supportsAllDrives occurrences (grep verified); README.md:13, CLAUDE.md:21 (stale DWD claim)
implementationPlan: 1) Add supportsAllDrives:true (and includeItemsFromAllDrives where applicable) to every Drive call in drive.ts (files.create x2, files.get x3, files.delete x2, permissions.create x2, list calls). 2) Move DRIVE_PARENT_FOLDER_ID to a folder on a Shared Drive the SA is a content-manager member of; verify permissions.create reader-sharing semantics still work for observed staff on Shared Drive items. 3) Add a scheduled function (e.g. apps/functions/src/drive/monitorDriveQuota.ts) that calls drive.about.get(storageQuota) while still on My Drive and emails the security admin past a threshold. 4) Correct README.md and CLAUDE.md to describe the real architecture (SA-owned / Shared Drive, no DWD).

title: Booking transaction commits before the Draft observation exists — a post-transaction failure strands the invitee as 'booked' with no observation and no retry path
currentState: bookObservationSlot commits the slot/ledger/invitee transaction first, then calls createDraftObservationForBooking outside any try/catch (apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:299-307). If observation creation or the separate slot.observationId write-back (:126-131) fails, the slot is Booked, the invitee has bookedSlotId set, peBusyIntervals contains the interval — but no observation document exists and slot.observationId is unset. The client sees an error; retrying throws 'You already have a booking in this window' (:239-241), so the staff member is permanently stuck unless an admin hand-edits the window doc. assignObservationFromPreference has the identical uncaught path (assignObservationFromPreference.ts:158-166). onObservationBooked (calendar events) also never fires since it triggers on observation creation.
expectedBehavior: Booking is atomic from the user's perspective: either the slot is booked AND the Draft observation exists (with slot.observationId linked), or neither — with a clear retryable error. No state requires manual Firestore surgery to recover.
evidence: apps/functions/src/scheduling/bookObservationSlot.ts:299-307 (uncaught createDraftObservationForBooking after committed transaction), :126-131 (slot link written in a separate non-transactional update), :239-241 (retry blocked); apps/functions/src/scheduling/assignObservationFromPreference.ts:158-166 (same pattern)
implementationPlan: 1) Move the observation doc creation and the slot.observationId write into the booking transaction itself (pre-read staff/invitee identity before the tx; the obsRef.set and slot update are plain tx writes), keeping only email/calendar/blocked-slot side effects post-commit and individually caught. 2) Alternatively (smaller diff): wrap createDraftObservationForBooking in try/catch with a compensating rollback (revert slot to available, remove busy interval, clear invitee.bookedSlotId) before rethrowing. 3) Apply the same shape in assignObservationFromPreference. 4) Add unit tests for the failure path (observation write throws → booking state fully reverted or fully consistent).

title: Admins have zero visibility into module completion — moduleProgress is written by staff but no admin report, per-module roster, or per-staff drill-in exists anywhere
currentState: moduleProgress docs (itemId, moduleId, status:'done', completedAt — packages/shared/src/schema/moduleItem.ts:48-53) are written from the staff dashboard and module page, but the only readers are the staff member's own StaffDashboardPage.tsx and ModulePage.tsx (grep verified). No admin surface shows who has completed which module items; the module admin pages (ModulesPage/ModuleBuilderPage) and StaffPersonPage show nothing. Rules allow admin reads of a single staff member's subcollection (firestore.rules:97-99) but there is no collection-group rule, so a cross-staff completion query would be denied even server-side rules-wise for clients. The entire point of assigning compliance/participation modules — knowing who finished — is unverifiable in-app.
expectedBehavior: An admin can open a module and see a completion roster (assigned staff × items, with completion dates), and see a staff member's module progress on their admin page. Matches what a professional compliance-tracking tool provides.
evidence: Grep of apps/web/src for 'moduleProgress' hits only ModulePage.tsx:40,60 and StaffDashboardPage.tsx:83-296 (no admin page); firestore.rules:97-99 (owner/admin per-staff rule only, no /{path=**}/moduleProgress collection-group rule)
implementationPlan: 1) Add a collection-group rules block match /{path=**}/moduleProgress/{itemId} { allow read: if isAdmin(); } (keep the per-owner rule) + rules tests. 2) Add a COLLECTION_GROUP index on moduleProgress(moduleId ASC, completedAt DESC) to firestore.indexes.json. 3) Build a completion view: a 'Progress' tab on ModuleBuilderPage (or new ModuleProgressPage) that queries collectionGroup('moduleProgress').where('moduleId','==',id), joins against the module's items and assigned staff (modules array + autoEnable match) to show done/not-done per person; derive the staff email from the doc's ref.parent.parent.id. 4) Optionally surface a per-staff module progress card on StaffPersonPage.

title: No Firestore offline persistence — a WiFi drop or tab reload mid-observation on the classroom iPad loses queued scripting/scoring writes silently
currentState: apps/web/src/lib/firebase.ts:28 initializes Firestore with plain getFirestore(firebaseApp) — no persistentLocalCache/IndexedDB persistence. During an in-classroom observation (the app's primary use case, on iPad per the Playwright viewports), a network drop means autosaved edits queue only in memory: a tab refresh, Safari tab eviction, or device sleep discards them with no warning, and all reads fail. There is no offline/online indicator or pending-writes state anywhere in the editor.
expectedBehavior: The editor keeps working through classroom WiFi drops: writes persist to IndexedDB and sync when connectivity returns, surviving reloads; the editor shows a clear offline/pending-sync indicator so the evaluator knows the state of their notes.
evidence: apps/web/src/lib/firebase.ts:28 (export const db = getFirestore(firebaseApp); no persistence configured); no occurrence of persistentLocalCache/enableIndexedDbPersistence anywhere in apps/web/src (grep verified)
implementationPlan: 1) Replace getFirestore with initializeFirestore(firebaseApp, { localCache: persistentLocalCache({ tabManager: persistentMultipleTabManager() }) }). 2) Add a connectivity/pending-writes indicator to ObservationEditorPage's save-status area (navigator.onLine + waitForPendingWrites or snapshot metadata.hasPendingWrites) so 'Saved' is not shown for unsynced local writes. 3) Verify the emulator connection path (connectFirestoreEmulator) still works with initializeFirestore ordering. 4) Smoke-test multi-tab behavior since admin pages may be open concurrently.

title: No Firestore backup, export, or disaster-recovery posture anywhere in the repo — evaluation records have no documented recovery path
currentState: Grep across README.md, docs/, .github/ and the whole repo finds zero mention of backups, PITR, or Firestore export. There is no scheduled export function, no gcloud backup-schedule config, and no runbook. The system stores multi-year, HR-adjacent staff evaluation records; several audited gaps show destructive paths exist (admin raw writes, delete flows, bulk edits, the destructive import:prod --force-overwrite at scripts/import/README.md:17-18), and a bad bulk operation or scripting accident is currently unrecoverable from anything in the repo.
expectedBehavior: Automated daily backups (Firestore scheduled backups or scheduled gcloud exports to a GCS bucket) with retention, plus a short documented restore procedure; cutover docs include a pre-import backup step.
evidence: grep -ril 'backup|pitr|point-in-time' over README.md, docs/, .github/ returns nothing; firebase.json and both deploy workflows contain no backup/export configuration; scripts/import/README.md:14-18 documents a destructive --force-overwrite prod import with no backup prerequisite
implementationPlan: 1) Enable PITR + a daily Firestore backup schedule on the project (gcloud firestore backups schedules create --recurrence=daily --retention=7w) and commit a docs/operations.md runbook covering restore, plus the Drive/Storage story. 2) Alternatively/additionally add a scheduled Cloud Function calling the Firestore export API to a versioned GCS bucket. 3) Add a 'take a backup first' gate to the prod import instructions and to the year-rollover tool plan. 4) Reference the runbook from README.md.
